[DEBUG] Review Content for FrontendDeveloper -> SystemArchitect:
  [design_spec.md] # OnlineLibrary Design Specification  ---  ## Section 1: Flask Routes Specification (Backend Development)  ### Route 1: Root Redirect - **URL path**: `/` - **Function name**: `root_redirect` - **HTTP method**: GET - **Template rendered**: None (Redirect) - **Behavior**: Redirects to the dashboard page.  ### Route 2: Dashboard Page - **URL path**: `/dashboard` - **Function name**: `dashboard` - **HTTP method**: GET - **Template rendered**: `dashboard.html` - **Context variables**:   - `username` (str): The logged-in user's username for welcome message display.  ### Route 3: Book Catalog Page - **URL path**: `/catalog` - **Function name**: `book_catalog` - **HTTP method**: GET - **Template rendered**: `catalog.html` - **Context variables**:   - `books` (list of dict): List of books with each dict containing:     - `book_id` (int)     - `title` (str)     - `author` (str)     - `status` (str)  ### Route 4: Book Details Page - **URL path**: `/book/<int:book_id>` - **Function name**: `book_details` - **HTTP method**: GET - **Template rendered**: `book_details.html` - **Context variables**:   - `book` (dict): Details of the book with fields:     - `book_id` (int)     - `title` (str)     - `author` (str)     - `status` (str)     - Additional fields if needed   - `reviews` (list of dict): List of reviews for the book, each dict:     - `review_id` (int)     - `username` (str)     - `rating` (int)     - `review_text` (str)     - `review_date` (str) ISO format  ### Route 5: Borrow Confirmation Page (GET) - **URL path**: `/borrow/<int:book_id>` - **Function name**: `borrow_confirmation_get` - **HTTP method**: GET - **Template rendered**: `borrow_confirmation.html` - **Context variables**:   - `book` (dict): Basic info about the book:     - `book_id` (int)     - `title` (str)   - `due_date` (str): Due date string for 14 days from current date  ### Route 6: Borrow Confirmation Page (POST) - **URL path**: `/borrow/<int:book_id>` - **Function name**: `borrow_confirmation_post` - **HTTP method**: POST - **Template rendered**: `borrow_confirmation.html` or redirect - **Context variables**: Same as GET for re-render on failure, otherwise redirect on success.  ### Route 7: My Borrowings Page - **URL path**: `/my-borrows` - **Function name**: `my_borrowings` - **HTTP method**: GET - **Template rendered**: `my_borrows.html` - **Context variables**:   - `borrowings` (list of dict): Each dict contains:     - `borrow_id` (int)     - `title` (str)     - `borrow_date` (str)     - `due_date` (str)     - `status` (str) (Active, Returned, Overdue)   - `filter_status` (str): Current filter value  ### Route 8: Return Book Action - **URL path**: `/return/<int:borrow_id>` - **Function name**: `return_book` - **HTTP method**: POST - **Template rendered**: Redirect back to `/my-borrows` after processing - **Context variables**: None  ### Route 9: My Reservations Page - **URL path**: `/my-reservations` - **Function name**: `my_reservations` - **HTTP method**: GET - **Template rendered**: `my_reservations.html` - **Context variables**:   - `reservations` (list of dict): Each dict contains:     - `reservation_id` (int)     - `title` (str)     - `reservation_date` (str)     - `status` (str)  ### Route 10: Cancel Reservation Action - **URL path**: `/cancel-reservation/<int:reservation_id>` - **Function name**: `cancel_reservation` - **HTTP method**: POST - **Template rendered**: Redirect back to `/my-reservations` - **Context variables**: None  ### Route 11: My Reviews Page - **URL path**: `/my-reviews` - **Function name**: `my_reviews` - **HTTP method**: GET - **Template rendered**: `my_reviews.html` - **Context variables**:   - `reviews` (list of dict): Each dict contains:     - `review_id` (int)     - `book_title` (str)     - `rating` (int)     - `review_text` (str)  ### Route 12: Edit Review Page (GET) - **URL path**: `/write-review/<int:book_id>` or `/edit-review/<int:review_id>` - **Function name**: `write_edit_review_get` - **HTTP method**: GET - **Template rendered**: `write_review.html` - **Context variables**:   - `book` (dict): with `book_id` (int) and title info   - `existing_review` (dict or None): if editing, includes `rating` (int), `review_text` (str)  ### Route 13: Submit Review (POST) - **URL path**: `/write-review/<int:book_id>` or `/edit-review/<int:review_id>` - **Function name**: `write_edit_review_post` - **HTTP method**: POST - **Template rendered**: Redirect back to book details page on success - **Context variables**: For form validation errors, re-render with error messages  ### Route 14: Delete Review Action - **URL path**: `/delete-review/<int:review_id>` - **Function name**: `delete_review` - **HTTP method**: POST - **Template rendered**: Redirect back to `/my-reviews` - **Context variables**: None  ### Route 15: User Profile Page - **URL path**: `/profile` - **Function name**: `user_profile` - **HTTP method**: GET - **Template rendered**: `profile.html` - **Context variables**:   - `username` (str)   - `email` (str)   - `borrow_history` (list of dict): List of all previously borrowed books:     - `title` (str)     - `borrow_date` (str)     - `return_date` (str or None)  ### Route 16: Update Profile (POST) - **URL path**: `/profile` - **Function name**: `update_profile` - **HTTP method**: POST - **Template rendered**: `profile.html`, possibly with success or error messages - **Context variables**: Same as GET  ### Route 17: Payment Confirmation Page - **URL path**: `/payment/<int:fine_id>` - **Function name**: `payment_confirmation_get` - **HTTP method**: GET - **Template rendered**: `payment_confirmation.html` - **Context variables**:   - `fine_amount` (float)   - `fine_id` (int)  ### Route 18: Confirm Payment (POST) - **URL path**: `/payment/<int:fine_id>` - **Function name**: `payment_confirmation_post` - **HTTP method**: POST - **Template rendered**: Redirect back to `/profile` on success - **Context variables**: None  ---  ## Section 2: HTML Template Specifications (Frontend Development)  ### Template 1: dashboard.html - **File path**: `templates/dashboard.html` - **Page Title**: "Library Dashboard" - **Elements**:   - `dashboard-page` (div)   - `welcome-message` (h1) - Displays welcome message with username   - `browse-books-button` (button) - Navigates to book catalog   - `my-borrows-button` (button) - Navigates to my borrowings - **Accessible Context Variables**:   - `username` (str) - **Navigation mappings**:   - `browse-books-button` onclick uses `url_for('book_catalog')`   - `my-borrows-button` onclick uses `url_for('my_borrowings')`  ### Template 2: catalog.html - **File path**: `templates/catalog.html` - **Page Title**: "Book Catalog" - **Elements**:   - `catalog-page` (div)   - `search-input` (input)   - `book-grid` (div) contains cards for each book with:     - Buttons with ID pattern: `view-book-button-{{ book.book_id }}` (button)   - `back-to-dashboard` (button) - **Accessible Context Variables**:   - `books` (list of dict): Each dict with keys: `book_id`, `title`, `author`, `status` - **Navigation mappings**:   - Each `view-book-button-{{ book.book_id }}` uses `url_for('book_details', book_id=book.book_id)`   - `back-to-dashboard` uses `url_for('dashboard')`  ### Template 3: book_details.html - **File path**: `templates/book_details.html` - **Page Title**: "Book Details" - **Elements**:   - `book-details-page` (div)   - `book-title` (h1)   - `book-author` (div)   - `book-status` (div)   - `borrow-button` (button)   - `reviews-section` (div) with all reviews   - `write-review-button` (button)   - `back-to-catalog` (button) - **Accessible Context Variables**:   - `book` (dict) with `book_id`, `title`, `author`, `status`   - `reviews` (list of dict): `review_id`, `username`, `rating`, `review_text`, `review_date` - **Navigation mappings**:   - `borrow-button` uses `url_for('borrow_confirmation_get', book_id=book.book_id)`   - `write-review-button` uses `url_for('write_edit_review_get', book_id=book.book_id)`   - `back-to-catalog` uses `url_for('book_catalog')`  ### Template 4: borrow_confirmation.html - **File path**: `templates/borrow_confirmation.html` - **Page Title**: "Borrow Confirmation" - **Elements**:   - `borrow-page` (div)   - `borrow-book-info` (div)   - `due-date-display` (div)   - `confirm-borrow-button` (button) - Form submission button   - `cancel-borrow-button` (button) - Navigates back - **Accessible Context Variables**:   - `book` (dict) with `book_id`, `title`   - `due_date` (str) - **Navigation mappings**:   - `confirm-borrow-button` in form POSTs to `/borrow/{{ book.book_id }}`   - `cancel-borrow-button` uses `url_for('book_details', book_id=book.book_id)`  ### Template 5: my_borrows.html - **File path**: `templates/my_borrows.html` - **Page Title**: "My Borrowings" - **Elements**:   - `my-borrows-page` (div)   - `filter-status` (dropdown)   - `borrows-table` (table) with rows containing:     - `return-book-button-{{ borrow.borrow_id }}` (button) for active borrows   - `back-to-dashboard` (button) - **Accessible Context Variables**:   - `borrowings` (list of dict): each dict with `borrow_id`, `title`, `borrow_date`, `due_date`, `status`   - `filter_status` (str) - **Navigation mappings**:   - `return-book-button-{{ borrow.borrow_id }}` form POST to `/return/{{ borrow.borrow_id }}`   - `back-to-dashboard` uses `url_for('dashboard')`  ### Template 6: my_reservations.html - **File path**: `templates/my_reservations.html` - **Page Title**: "My Reservations" - **Elements**:   - `reservations-page` (div)   - `reservations-table` (table) with rows containing:     - `cancel-reservation-button-{{ reservation.reservation_id }}` (button)   - `back-to-dashboard` (button) - **Accessible Context Variables**:   - `reservations` (list of dict): with `reservation_id`, `title`, `reservation_date`, `status` - **Navigation mappings**:   - `cancel-reservation-button-{{ reservation.reservation_id }}` form POST to `/cancel-reservation/{{ reservation.reservation_id }}`   - `back-to-dashboard` uses `url_for('dashboard')`  ### Template 7: my_reviews.html - **File path**: `templates/my_reviews.html` - **Page Title**: "My Reviews" - **Elements**:   - `reviews-page` (div)   - `reviews-list` (div) with each review including:     - `edit-review-button-{{ review.review_id }}` (button)     - `delete-review-button-{{ review.review_id }}` (button)   - `back-to-dashboard` (button) - **Accessible Context Variables**:   - `reviews` (list of dict) with `review_id`, `book_title`, `rating`, `review_text` - **Navigation mappings**:   - `edit-review-button-{{ review.review_id }}` uses `url_for('write_edit_review_get', book_id=None)` or routed via review_id   - `delete-review-button-{{ review.review_id }}` form POST to `/delete-review/{{ review.review_id }}`   - `back-to-dashboard` uses `url_for('dashboard')`  ### Template 8: write_review.html - **File path**: `templates/write_review.html` - **Page Title**: "Write Review" - **Elements**:   - `write-review-page` (div)   - `book-info-display` (div)   - `rating-input` (dropdown, 1-5 stars)   - `review-text` (textarea)   - `submit-review-button` (button)   - `back-to-book` (button) - **Accessible Context Variables**:   - `book` (dict): with `book_id`, `title`   - `existing_review` (dict or None): with `rating`, `review_text` if editing - **Navigation mappings**:   - `submit-review-button` submits form POST to `/write-review/{{ book.book_id }}` or `/edit-review/{{ existing_review.review_id }}`   - `back-to-book` uses `url_for('book_details', book_id=book.book_id)`  ### Template 9: profile.html - **File path**: `templates/profile.html` - **Page Title**: "My Profile" - **Elements**:   - `profile-page` (div)   - `profile-username` (div)   - `profile-email` (input)   - `update-profile-button` (button)   - `borrow-history` (div)   - `back-to-dashboard` (button) - **Accessible Context Variables**:   - `username` (str)   - `email` (str)   - `borrow_history` (list of dict): each with `title`, `borrow_date`, `return_date` - **Navigation mappings**:   - `update-profile-button` triggers POST to `/profile`   - `back-to-dashboard` uses `url_for('dashboard')`  ### Template 10: payment_confirmation.html - **File path**: `templates/payment_confirmation.html` - **Page Title**: "Payment Confirmation" - **Elements**:   - `payment-page` (div)   - `fine-amount-display` (div)   - `confirm-payment-button` (button)   - `back-to-profile` (button) - **Accessible Context Variables**:   - `fine_amount` (float)   - `fine_id` (int) - **Navigation mappings**:   - `confirm-payment-button` submits POST to `/payment/{{ fine_id }}`   - `back-to-profile` uses `url_for('user_profile')`  ---  ## Section 3: Data File Schemas (Backend Development)  ### 1. users.txt - **Path**: `data/users.txt` - **Fields (pipe-delimited)**:   1. `username` (str)   2. `email` (str)   3. `phone` (str)   4. `address` (str) - **Description**: Stores user account data with contact details. - **Example rows**:   ```   john_reader|john@example.com|555-1234|123 Main St   jane_doe|jane@example.com|555-5678|789 Oak St   ``` - **Notes**: No header line. All fields are strings.  ### 2. books.txt - **Path**: `data/books.txt` - **Fields (pipe-delimited)**:   1. `book_id` (int)   2. `title` (str)   3. `author` (str)   4. `isbn` (str)   5. `genre` (str)   6. `publisher` (str)   7. `year` (int)   8. `description` (str)   9. `status` (str) - Available, Borrowed, Reserved   10. `avg_rating` (float) - **Description**: Stores complete book metadata including availability. - **Example rows**:   ```   1|To Kill a Mockingbird|Harper Lee|978-0-06-112008-4|Fiction|HarperCollins|1960|A gripping tale of racial injustice and childhood innocence.|Available|4.8   2|Sapiens|Yuval Noah Harari|978-0-06-231609-7|Non-Fiction|Harper|2011|A brief history of humankind.|Borrowed|4.6   3|1984|George Orwell|978-0-452-28423-4|Fiction|Signet Classic|1949|A dystopian social science fiction novel.|Available|4.7   ``` - **Notes**: Pipe delimiter, no header. Fields must be parsed in order.  ### 3. borrowings.txt - **Path**: `data/borrowings.txt` - **Fields (pipe-delimited)**:   1. `borrow_id` (int)   2. `username` (str)   3. `book_id` (int)   4. `borrow_date` (str, ISO date `YYYY-MM-DD`)   5. `due_date` (str, ISO date)   6. `return_date` (str or empty, ISO date)   7. `status` (str) - Active, Returned, Overdue   8. `fine_amount` (float) - **Description**: Records each borrowing transaction details, status, and fines if any. - **Example rows**:   ```   1|john_reader|2|2024-11-01|2024-11-15||Active|0   2|jane_doe|1|2024-10-20|2024-11-03|2024-11-02|Returned|0   3|john_reader|3|2024-10-15|2024-10-29||Overdue|5.00   ``` - **Notes**: Empty `return_date` indicates not returned yet.  ### 4. reservations.txt - **Path**: `data/reservations.txt` - **Fields (pipe-delimited)**:   1. `reservation_id` (int)   2. `username` (str)   3. `book_id` (int)   4. `reservation_date` (str, ISO date)   5. `status` (str) - Active, Cancelled - **Description**: Tracks book reservations made by users. - **Example rows**:   ```   1|jane_doe|4|2024-11-10|Active   2|john_reader|2|2024-10-25|Cancelled   ``` - **Notes**: Status reflects current valid or cancelled reservations.  ### 5. reviews.txt - **Path**: `data/reviews.txt` - **Fields (pipe-delimited)**:   1. `review_id` (int)   2. `username` (str)   3. `book_id` (int)   4. `rating` (int, 1-5)   5. `review_text` (str)   6. `review_date` (str, ISO date) - **Description**: Stores user reviews and ratings for books. - **Example rows**:   ```   1|jane_doe|1|5|An absolute masterpiece! A must-read.|2024-11-03   2|john_reader|3|4|Thought-provoking and eerily relevant today.|2024-10-20   ``` - **Notes**: Ratings limited to integers 1 through 5.  ### 6. fines.txt - **Path**: `data/fines.txt` - **Fields (pipe-delimited)**:   1. `fine_id` (int)   2. `username` (str)   3. `borrow_id` (int)   4. `amount` (float)   5. `status` (str) - Unpaid, Paid   6. `date_issued` (str, ISO date) - **Description**: Records fines owed or paid by users for overdue borrowings. - **Example rows**:   ```   1|john_reader|3|5.00|Unpaid|2024-10-30   ``` - **Notes**: Status flags whether fine is paid or unpaid.  ---  This specification document fully supports independent backend and frontend development with precise route, template, and data schema definitions per user requirements.

# OnlineLibrary Design Specification

---

## Section 1: Flask Routes Specification (Backend Development)

### Route 1: Root Redirect
- **URL path**: `/`
- **Function name**: `root_redirect`
- **HTTP method**: GET
- **Template rendered**: None (Redirect)
- **Behavior**: Redirects to the dashboard page.

### Route 2: Dashboard Page
- **URL path**: `/dashboard`
- **Function name**: `dashboard`
- **HTTP method**: GET
- **Template rendered**: `dashboard.html`
- **Context variables**:
  - `username` (str): The logged-in user's username for welcome message display.

### Route 3: Book Catalog Page
- **URL path**: `/catalog`
- **Function name**: `book_catalog`
- **HTTP method**: GET
- **Template rendered**: `catalog.html`
- **Context variables**:
  - `books` (list of dict): List of books with each dict containing:
    - `book_id` (int)
    - `title` (str)
    - `author` (str)
    - `status` (str)

### Route 4: Book Details Page
- **URL path**: `/book/<int:book_id>`
- **Function name**: `book_details`
- **HTTP method**: GET
- **Template rendered**: `book_details.html`
- **Context variables**:
  - `book` (dict): Details of the book with fields:
    - `book_id` (int)
    - `title` (str)
    - `author` (str)
    - `status` (str)
    - Additional fields if needed
  - `reviews` (list of dict): List of reviews for the book, each dict:
    - `review_id` (int)
    - `username` (str)
    - `rating` (int)
    - `review_text` (str)
    - `review_date` (str) ISO format

### Route 5: Borrow Confirmation Page (GET)
- **URL path**: `/borrow/<int:book_id>`
- **Function name**: `borrow_confirmation_get`
- **HTTP method**: GET
- **Template rendered**: `borrow_confirmation.html`
- **Context variables**:
  - `book` (dict): Basic info about the book:
    - `book_id` (int)
    - `title` (str)
  - `due_date` (str): Due date string for 14 days from current date

### Route 6: Borrow Confirmation Page (POST)
- **URL path**: `/borrow/<int:book_id>`
- **Function name**: `borrow_confirmation_post`
- **HTTP method**: POST
- **Template rendered**: `borrow_confirmation.html` or redirect
- **Context variables**: Same as GET for re-render on failure, otherwise redirect on success.

### Route 7: My Borrowings Page
- **URL path**: `/my-borrows`
- **Function name**: `my_borrowings`
- **HTTP method**: GET
- **Template rendered**: `my_borrows.html`
- **Context variables**:
  - `borrowings` (list of dict): Each dict contains:
    - `borrow_id` (int)
    - `title` (str)
    - `borrow_date` (str)
    - `due_date` (str)
    - `status` (str) (Active, Returned, Overdue)
  - `filter_status` (str): Current filter value

### Route 8: Return Book Action
- **URL path**: `/return/<int:borrow_id>`
- **Function name**: `return_book`
- **HTTP method**: POST
- **Template rendered**: Redirect back to `/my-borrows` after processing
- **Context variables**: None

### Route 9: My Reservations Page
- **URL path**: `/my-reservations`
- **Function name**: `my_reservations`
- **HTTP method**: GET
- **Template rendered**: `my_reservations.html`
- **Context variables**:
  - `reservations` (list of dict): Each dict contains:
    - `reservation_id` (int)
    - `title` (str)
    - `reservation_date` (str)
    - `status` (str)

### Route 10: Cancel Reservation Action
- **URL path**: `/cancel-reservation/<int:reservation_id>`
- **Function name**: `cancel_reservation`
- **HTTP method**: POST
- **Template rendered**: Redirect back to `/my-reservations`
- **Context variables**: None

### Route 11: My Reviews Page
- **URL path**: `/my-reviews`
- **Function name**: `my_reviews`
- **HTTP method**: GET
- **Template rendered**: `my_reviews.html`
- **Context variables**:
  - `reviews` (list of dict): Each dict contains:
    - `review_id` (int)
    - `book_title` (str)
    - `rating` (int)
    - `review_text` (str)

### Route 12: Edit Review Page (GET)
- **URL path**: `/write-review/<int:book_id>` or `/edit-review/<int:review_id>`
- **Function name**: `write_edit_review_get`
- **HTTP method**: GET
- **Template rendered**: `write_review.html`
- **Context variables**:
  - `book` (dict): with `book_id` (int) and title info
  - `existing_review` (dict or None): if editing, includes `rating` (int), `review_text` (str)

### Route 13: Submit Review (POST)
- **URL path**: `/write-review/<int:book_id>` or `/edit-review/<int:review_id>`
- **Function name**: `write_edit_review_post`
- **HTTP method**: POST
- **Template rendered**: Redirect back to book details page on success
- **Context variables**: For form validation errors, re-render with error messages

### Route 14: Delete Review Action
- **URL path**: `/delete-review/<int:review_id>`
- **Function name**: `delete_review`
- **HTTP method**: POST
- **Template rendered**: Redirect back to `/my-reviews`
- **Context variables**: None

### Route 15: User Profile Page
- **URL path**: `/profile`
- **Function name**: `user_profile`
- **HTTP method**: GET
- **Template rendered**: `profile.html`
- **Context variables**:
  - `username` (str)
  - `email` (str)
  - `borrow_history` (list of dict): List of all previously borrowed books:
    - `title` (str)
    - `borrow_date` (str)
    - `return_date` (str or None)

### Route 16: Update Profile (POST)
- **URL path**: `/profile`
- **Function name**: `update_profile`
- **HTTP method**: POST
- **Template rendered**: `profile.html`, possibly with success or error messages
- **Context variables**: Same as GET

### Route 17: Payment Confirmation Page
- **URL path**: `/payment/<int:fine_id>`
- **Function name**: `payment_confirmation_get`
- **HTTP method**: GET
- **Template rendered**: `payment_confirmation.html`
- **Context variables**:
  - `fine_amount` (float)
  - `fine_id` (int)

### Route 18: Confirm Payment (POST)
- **URL path**: `/payment/<int:fine_id>`
- **Function name**: `payment_confirmation_post`
- **HTTP method**: POST
- **Template rendered**: Redirect back to `/profile` on success
- **Context variables**: None

---

## Section 2: HTML Template Specifications (Frontend Development)

### Template 1: dashboard.html
- **File path**: `templates/dashboard.html`
- **Page Title**: "Library Dashboard"
- **Elements**:
  - `dashboard-page` (div)
  - `welcome-message` (h1) - Displays welcome message with username
  - `browse-books-button` (button) - Navigates to book catalog
  - `my-borrows-button` (button) - Navigates to my borrowings
- **Accessible Context Variables**:
  - `username` (str)
- **Navigation mappings**:
  - `browse-books-button` onclick uses `url_for('book_catalog')`
  - `my-borrows-button` onclick uses `url_for('my_borrowings')`

### Template 2: catalog.html
- **File path**: `templates/catalog.html`
- **Page Title**: "Book Catalog"
- **Elements**:
  - `catalog-page` (div)
  - `search-input` (input)
  - `book-grid` (div) contains cards for each book with:
    - Buttons with ID pattern: `view-book-button-{{ book.book_id }}` (button)
  - `back-to-dashboard` (button)
- **Accessible Context Variables**:
  - `books` (list of dict): Each dict with keys: `book_id`, `title`, `author`, `status`
- **Navigation mappings**:
  - Each `view-book-button-{{ book.book_id }}` uses `url_for('book_details', book_id=book.book_id)`
  - `back-to-dashboard` uses `url_for('dashboard')`

### Template 3: book_details.html
- **File path**: `templates/book_details.html`
- **Page Title**: "Book Details"
- **Elements**:
  - `book-details-page` (div)
  - `book-title` (h1)
  - `book-author` (div)
  - `book-status` (div)
  - `borrow-button` (button)
  - `reviews-section` (div) with all reviews
  - `write-review-button` (button)
  - `back-to-catalog` (button)
- **Accessible Context Variables**:
  - `book` (dict) with `book_id`, `title`, `author`, `status`
  - `reviews` (list of dict): `review_id`, `username`, `rating`, `review_text`, `review_date`
- **Navigation mappings**:
  - `borrow-button` uses `url_for('borrow_confirmation_get', book_id=book.book_id)`
  - `write-review-button` uses `url_for('write_edit_review_get', book_id=book.book_id)`
  - `back-to-catalog` uses `url_for('book_catalog')`

- **File path**: `templates/borrow_confirmation.html`
- **Page Title**: "Borrow Confirmation"
- **Elements**:
  - `borrow-page` (div)
  - `borrow-book-info` (div)
  - `due-date-display` (div)
  - `confirm-borrow-button` (button) - Form submission button
  - `cancel-borrow-button` (button) - Navigates back
- **Accessible Context Variables**:
  - `book` (dict) with `book_id`, `title`
  - `due_date` (str)
- **Navigation mappings**:
  - `confirm-borrow-button` in form POSTs to `/borrow/{{ book.book_id }}`
  - `cancel-borrow-button` uses `url_for('book_details', book_id=book.book_id)`

- **Page Title**: "My Borrowings"
- **Elements**:
  - `my-borrows-page` (div)
  - `filter-status` (dropdown)
  - `borrows-table` (table) with rows containing:
    - `return-book-button-{{ borrow.borrow_id }}` (button) for active borrows
  - `back-to-dashboard` (button)
- **Accessible Context Variables**:
  - `borrowings` (list of dict): each dict with `borrow_id`, `title`, `borrow_date`, `due_date`, `status`

- **Navigation mappings**:
  - `return-book-button-{{ borrow.borrow_id }}` form POST to `/return/{{ borrow.borrow_id }}`
  - `back-to-dashboard` uses `url_for('dashboard')`

  - `reservations-page` (div)
  - `reservations-table` (table) with rows containing:
    - `cancel-reservation-button-{{ reservation.reservation_id }}` (button)

- **Accessible Context Variables**:
  - `reservations` (list of dict): with `reservation_id`, `title`, `reservation_date`, `status`

  - `back-to-dashboard` uses `url_for('dashboard')`

    - `edit-review-button-{{ review.review_id }}` (button)

- **Navigation mappings**:
  - `edit-review-button-{{ review.review_id }}` uses `url_for('write_edit_review_get', book_id=None)` or routed via review_id
  - `delete-review-button-{{ review.review_id }}` form POST to `/delete-review/{{ review.review_id }}`
  - `back-to-dashboard` uses `url_for('dashboard')`

- **Navigation mappings**:
  - `submit-review-button` submits form POST to `/write-review/{{ book.book_id }}` or `/edit-review/{{ existing_review.review_id }}`
  - `back-to-book` uses `url_for('book_details', book_id=book.book_id)`

  - `confirm-payment-button` submits POST to `/payment/{{ fine_id }}`
  - `back-to-profile` uses `url_for('user_profile')`

---

## Section 3: Data File Schemas (Backend Development)

### 1. users.txt
- **Path**: `data/users.txt`
- **Fields (pipe-delimited)**:
  1. `username` (str)
  2. `email` (str)
  3. `phone` (str)
  4. `address` (str)
- **Description**: Stores user account data with contact details.
- **Example rows**:
  ```
  john_reader|john@example.com|555-1234|123 Main St
  jane_doe|jane@example.com|555-5678|789 Oak St
  ```
- **Notes**: No header line. All fields are strings.

### 2. books.txt
- **Path**: `data/books.txt`
- **Fields (pipe-delimited)**:
  1. `book_id` (int)
  2. `title` (str)
  3. `author` (str)
  4. `isbn` (str)
  5. `genre` (str)
  6. `publisher` (str)
  7. `year` (int)
  8. `description` (str)
  9. `status` (str) - Available, Borrowed, Reserved
  10. `avg_rating` (float)
- **Description**: Stores complete book metadata including availability.
- **Example rows**:
  ```
  1|To Kill a Mockingbird|Harper Lee|978-0-06-112008-4|Fiction|HarperCollins|1960|A gripping tale of racial injustice and childhood innocence.|Available|4.8
  2|Sapiens|Yuval Noah Harari|978-0-06-231609-7|Non-Fiction|Harper|2011|A brief history of humankind.|Borrowed|4.6
  3|1984|George Orwell|978-0-452-28423-4|Fiction|Signet Classic|1949|A dystopian social science fiction novel.|Available|4.7
  ```
- **Notes**: Pipe delimiter, no header. Fields must be parsed in order.

### 3. borrowings.txt
- **Path**: `data/borrowings.txt`
- **Fields (pipe-delimited)**:
  1. `borrow_id` (int)
  2. `username` (str)
  3. `book_id` (int)
  4. `borrow_date` (str, ISO date `YYYY-MM-DD`)
  5. `due_date` (str, ISO date)
  6. `return_date` (str or empty, ISO date)
  7. `status` (str) - Active, Returned, Overdue
  8. `fine_amount` (float)
- **Description**: Records each borrowing transaction details, status, and fines if any.
- **Example rows**:
  ```
  1|john_reader|2|2024-11-01|2024-11-15||Active|0
  2|jane_doe|1|2024-10-20|2024-11-03|2024-11-02|Returned|0
  3|john_reader|3|2024-10-15|2024-10-29||Overdue|5.00
  ```
- **Notes**: Empty `return_date` indicates not returned yet.

### 4. reservations.txt
- **Path**: `data/reservations.txt`
- **Fields (pipe-delimited)**:
  1. `reservation_id` (int)
  2. `username` (str)
  3. `book_id` (int)
  4. `reservation_date` (str, ISO date)
  5. `status` (str) - Active, Cancelled
- **Description**: Tracks book reservations made by users.
- **Example rows**:
  ```
  1|jane_doe|4|2024-11-10|Active
  2|john_reader|2|2024-10-25|Cancelled
  ```
- **Notes**: Status reflects current valid or cancelled reservations.

### 5. reviews.txt
- **Path**: `data/reviews.txt`
- **Fields (pipe-delimited)**:
  1. `review_id` (int)
  2. `username` (str)
  3. `book_id` (int)
  4. `rating` (int, 1-5)
  5. `review_text` (str)
  6. `review_date` (str, ISO date)
- **Description**: Stores user reviews and ratings for books.
- **Example rows**:
  ```
  1|jane_doe|1|5|An absolute masterpiece! A must-read.|2024-11-03
  2|john_reader|3|4|Thought-provoking and eerily relevant today.|2024-10-20
  ```
- **Notes**: Ratings limited to integers 1 through 5.

### 6. fines.txt
- **Path**: `data/fines.txt`
- **Fields (pipe-delimited)**:
  1. `fine_id` (int)
  2. `username` (str)
  3. `borrow_id` (int)
  4. `amount` (float)
  5. `status` (str) - Unpaid, Paid
  6. `date_issued` (str, ISO date)
- **Description**: Records fines owed or paid by users for overdue borrowings.
- **Example rows**:
  ```
  1|john_reader|3|5.00|Unpaid|2024-10-30
  ```
- **Notes**: Status flags whether fine is paid or unpaid.

---

This specification document fully supports independent backend and frontend development with precise route, template, and data schema definitions per user requirements.

        <div id="book-author">Author: {{ book.author }}</div>
        <div id="book-status">Status: {{ book.status }}</div>
        <button id="borrow-button" onclick="window.location.href='{{ url_for('borrow_confirmation_get', book_id=book.book_id) }}'">Borrow</button>
        <div id="reviews-section">
            <h2>Reviews</h2>
            {% if reviews %}
            <ul>
                {% for review in reviews %}
                <li>
                    <strong>{{ review.username }}</strong> ({{ review.review_date }}): 
                    Rating: {{ review.rating }} / 5
                    <p>{{ review.review_text }}</p>
                </li>
                {% endfor %}
            </ul>
            {% else %}
            <p>No reviews yet.</p>
            {% endif %}
        </div>
        <button id="write-review-button" onclick="window.location.href='{{ url_for('write_edit_review_get', book_id=book.book_id) }}'">Write Review</button>
        <button id="back-to-catalog" onclick="window.location.href='{{ url_for('book_catalog') }}'">Back to Catalog</button>
    </div>
</body>
</html>

            <option value="Active" {% if filter_status == 'Active' %}selected{% endif %}>Active</option>
            <option value="Returned" {% if filter_status == 'Returned' %}selected{% endif %}>Returned</option>
            <option value="Overdue" {% if filter_status == 'Overdue' %}selected{% endif %}>Overdue</option>
        </select>
        <table id="borrows-table" border="1">

                <tr>
                    <th>Title</th>
                    <th>Borrow Date</th>
                    <th>Due Date</th>
                    <th>Status</th>
                    <th>Action</th>
                </tr>
            </thead>

                <tr>
                    <td>{{ borrow.title }}</td>
                    <td>{{ borrow.borrow_date }}</td>
                    <td>{{ borrow.due_date }}</td>
                    <td>{{ borrow.status }}</td>
                    <td>
                    {% if borrow.status == 'Active' %}
                        <form method="POST" action="{{ url_for('return_book', borrow_id=borrow.borrow_id) }}">
                            <button id="return-book-button-{{ borrow.borrow_id }}" type="submit">Return Book</button>
                        </form>
                    {% else %}
                        N/A
                    {% endif %}
                    </td>
                </tr>
                {% endfor %}
            </tbody>
        </table>
        <button id="back-to-dashboard" onclick="window.location.href='{{ url_for('dashboard') }}'">Back to Dashboard</button>
    </div>
</body>
</html>

redirect(url_for('my_reviews'))\n        book_brief = {'book_id': book['book_id'], 'title': book['title']}\n        existing_review = {\n            'review_id': review['review_id'],\n            'rating': review['rating'],\n            'review_text': review['review_text']\n        }\n        return render_template('write_review.html', book=book_brief, existing_review=existing_review)\n\n    # If neither, redirect to dashboard\n    return redirect(url_for('dashboard'))\n\n# Route 13: Write/Edit Review (POST)\n@app.route('/write-review/<int:book_id>', methods=['POST'])\n@app.route('/edit-review/<int:review_id>', methods=['POST'])\ndef write_edit_review_post(book_id=None, review_id=None):\n    rating = request.form.get('rating')\n    review_text = request.form.get('review_text')\n\n    # Validation\n    error = None\n    try:\n        rating_int = int(rating)\n        if rating_int < 1 or rating_int > 5:\n            error = 'Rating must be between 1 and 5.'\n    except:\n        error = 'Invalid rating value.'\n\n    if not review_text or review_text.strip() == '':\n        error = 'Review text cannot be empty.'\n\n    books = read_books()\n    reviews = read_reviews()\n\n    if book_id is not None:\n        # Creating new review\n        if book_id not in books:\n            flash('Book not found.', 'error')\n            return redirect(url_for('book_catalog'))\n\n        # Check if user already has review\n        existing = None\n        for r in reviews.values():\n            if r['book_id'] == book_id and r['username'] == LOGGED_IN_USERNAME:\n                existing = r\n                break\n        if existing:\n            flash('Review already exists. Please edit it instead.', 'error')\n            return redirect(url_for('write_edit_review_get', review_id=existing['review_id']))\n\n        if error:\n            book_brief = {'book_id': book_id, 'title': books[book_id]['title']}\n            return render_template('write_review.html', book=book_brief, existing_review=None, error=error)\n\n        # Add new review\n        new_review_id = max(reviews.keys()) + 1 if reviews else 1\n        today_str = date_to_str(get_current_date())\n\n        new_review = {\n            'review_id': new_review_id,\n            'username': LOGGED_IN_USERNAME,\n            'book_id': book_id,\n            'rating': rating_int,\n            'review_text': review_text.strip(),\n            'review_date': today_str\n        }\n        reviews[new_review_id] = new_review\n\n        # Update average rating of book\n        update_book_avg_rating(book_id, reviews, books)\n\n        write_reviews(reviews)\n        write_books(books)\n\n        flash('Review added successfully.', 'success')\n        return redirect(url_for('book_details', book_id=book_id))\n\n    if review_id is not None:\n        # Editing existing review\n        if review_id not in reviews:\n            flash('Review not found.', 'error')\n            return redirect(url_for('my_reviews'))\n        review = reviews[review_id]\n\n        if review['username'] != LOGGED_IN_USERNAME:\n            flash('You can only edit your own reviews.', 'error')\n            return redirect(url_for('my_reviews'))\n\n        if error:\n            book = books.get(review['book_id'])\n            if not book:\n                flash('Book not found.', 'error')\n                return redirect(url_for('my_reviews'))\n            book_brief = {'book_id': book['book_id'], 'title': book['title']}\n            existing_review = {'rating': rating_int if not error else None, 'review_text': review_text}\n            return render_template('write_review.html', book=book_brief, existing_review=existing_review, error=error)\n\n        # Update data\n        review['rating'] = rating_int\n        review['review_text'] = review_text.strip()\n        review['review_date'] = date_to_str(get_current_date())\n\n        # Update average rating\n        update_book_avg_rating(review['book_id'], reviews, books)\n\n        reviews[review_id] = review\n        write_reviews(reviews)\n        write_books(books)\n\n        flash('Review updated successfully.', 'success')\n        return redirect(url_for('book_details', book_id=review['book_id']))\n\n    return redirect(url_for('dashboard'))\n\n# Utility to update book average rating\n\ndef update_book_avg_rating(book_id, reviews, books):\n    ratings = [r['rating'] for r in reviews.values() if r['book_id'] == book_id]\n    if ratings:\n        avg = sum(ratings) / len(ratings)\n    else:\n        avg = 0.0\n    book = books.get(book_id)\n    if book:\n        book['avg_rating'] = round(avg, 1)\n\n# Route 14: Delete Review\n@app.route('/delete-review/<int:review_id>', methods=['POST'])\ndef delete_review(review_id):\n    reviews = read_reviews()\n\n    if review_id not in reviews:\n        flash('Review not found.', 'error')\n        return redirect(url_for('my_reviews'))\n\n    review = reviews[review_id]\n    if review['username'] != LOGGED_IN_USERNAME:\n        flash('You can only delete your own reviews.', 'error')\n        return redirect(url_for('my_reviews'))\n\n    book_id = review['book_id']\n    del reviews[review_id]\n\n    # After delete, update average rating\n    books = read_books()\n    update_book_avg_rating(book_id, reviews, books)\n\n    write_reviews(reviews)\n    write_books(books)\n\n    flash('Review deleted successfully.', 'success')\n    return redirect(url_for('my_reviews'))\n\n# Route 15: User Profile (GET)\n@app.route('/profile', methods=['GET'])\ndef user_profile():\n    users = read_users()\n    borrowings = read_borrowings()\n    books = read_books()\n\n    if LOGGED_IN_USERNAME not in users:\n        flash('User not found.', 'error')\n        return redirect(url_for('dashboard'))\n\n    user = users[LOGGED_IN_USERNAME]\n    # Borrow history = all borrowings that user had\n    borrow_history = []\n    for b in borrowings.values():\n        if b['username'] == LOGGED_IN_USERNAME:\n            book = books.get(b['book_id'])\n            title = book['title'] if book else 'Unknown'\n            borrow_history.append({\n                'title': title,\n                'borrow_date': b['borrow_date'],\n                'return_date': b['return_date']\n            })\n\n    return render_template('profile.html', username=LOGGED_IN_USERNAME, email=user['email'], borrow_history=borrow_history)\n\n# Route 16: Update Profile (POST)\n@app.route('/profile', methods=['POST'])\ndef update_profile():\n    users = read_users()\n\n    if LOGGED_IN_USERNAME not in users:\n        flash('User not found.', 'error')\n        return redirect(url_for('dashboard'))\n\n    user = users[LOGGED_IN_USERNAME]\n\n    new_email = request.form.get('email', '').strip()\n    new_phone = request.form.get('phone', '').strip()\n    new_address = request.form.get('address', '').strip()\n\n    # Validate email\n    if not new_email:\n        flash('Email cannot be empty.', 'error')\n        return redirect(url_for('user_profile'))\n\n    # Update\n    user['email'] = new_email\n    user['phone'] = new_phone\n    user['address'] = new_address\n    users[LOGGED_IN_USERNAME] = user\n\n    write_users(users)\n    flash('Profile updated successfully.', 'success')\n\n    return redirect(url_for('user_profile'))\n\n# Route 17: Payment Confirmation (GET)\n@app.route('/payment/<int:fine_id>', methods=['GET'])\ndef payment_confirmation_get(fine_id):\n    fines = read_fines()\n\n    if fine_id not in fines:\n        flash('Fine record not found.', 'error')\n        return redirect(url_for('user_profile'))\n\n    fine = fines[fine_id]\n    if fine['username'] != LOGGED_IN_USERNAME:\n        flash('You can only pay your own fines.', 'error')\n        return redirect(url_for('user_profile'))\n\n    fine_amount = fine['amount']\n    return render_template('payment_confirmation.html', fine_amount=fine_amount, fine_id=fine_id)\n\n# Route 18: Confirm Payment (POST)\n@app.route('/payment/<int:fine_id>', methods=['POST'])\ndef payment_confirmation_post(fine_id):\n    fines = read_fines()\n\n    if fine_id not in fines:\n        flash('Fine record not found.', 'error')\n        return redirect(url_for('user_profile'))\n\n    fine = fines[fine_id]\n    if fine['username'] != LOGGED_IN_USERNAME:\n        flash('You can only pay your own fines.', 'error')\n        return redirect(url_for('user_profile'))\n\n    if fine['status'] == 'Paid':\n        flash('This fine is already paid.', 'info')\n        return redirect(url_for('user_profile'))\n\n    # Mark fine as paid\n    fine['status'] = 'Paid'\n    fines[fine_id] = fine\n    write_fines(fines)\n\n    flash('Payment successful. Thank you!', 'success')\n    return redirect(url_for('user_profile'))\n\n\nif __name__ == '__main__':\n    app.run(debug=True)\n"})

[DEBUG] Review Content for BackendDeveloper -> SystemArchitect:
  [design_spec.md] # OnlineLibrary Design Specification  ---  ## Section 1: Flask Routes Specification (Backend Development)  ### Route 1: Root Redirect - **URL path**: `/` - **Function name**: `root_redirect` - **HTTP method**: GET - **Template rendered**: None (Redirect) - **Behavior**: Redirects to the dashboard page.  ### Route 2: Dashboard Page - **URL path**: `/dashboard` - **Function name**: `dashboard` - **HTTP method**: GET - **Template rendered**: `dashboard.html` - **Context variables**:   - `username` (str): The logged-in user's username for welcome message display.  ### Route 3: Book Catalog Page - **URL path**: `/catalog` - **Function name**: `book_catalog` - **HTTP method**: GET - **Template rendered**: `catalog.html` - **Context variables**:   - `books` (list of dict): List of books with each dict containing:     - `book_id` (int)     - `title` (str)     - `author` (str)     - `status` (str)  ### Route 4: Book Details Page - **URL path**: `/book/<int:book_id>` - **Function name**: `book_details` - **HTTP method**: GET - **Template rendered**: `book_details.html` - **Context variables**:   - `book` (dict): Details of the book with fields:     - `book_id` (int)     - `title` (str)     - `author` (str)     - `status` (str)     - Additional fields if needed   - `reviews` (list of dict): List of reviews for the book, each dict:     - `review_id` (int)     - `username` (str)     - `rating` (int)     - `review_text` (str)     - `review_date` (str) ISO format  ### Route 5: Borrow Confirmation Page (GET) - **URL path**: `/borrow/<int:book_id>` - **Function name**: `borrow_confirmation_get` - **HTTP method**: GET - **Template rendered**: `borrow_confirmation.html` - **Context variables**:   - `book` (dict): Basic info about the book:     - `book_id` (int)     - `title` (str)   - `due_date` (str): Due date string for 14 days from current date  ### Route 6: Borrow Confirmation Page (POST) - **URL path**: `/borrow/<int:book_id>` - **Function name**: `borrow_confirmation_post` - **HTTP method**: POST - **Template rendered**: `borrow_confirmation.html` or redirect - **Context variables**: Same as GET for re-render on failure, otherwise redirect on success.  ### Route 7: My Borrowings Page - **URL path**: `/my-borrows` - **Function name**: `my_borrowings` - **HTTP method**: GET - **Template rendered**: `my_borrows.html` - **Context variables**:   - `borrowings` (list of dict): Each dict contains:     - `borrow_id` (int)     - `title` (str)     - `borrow_date` (str)     - `due_date` (str)     - `status` (str) (Active, Returned, Overdue)   - `filter_status` (str): Current filter value  ### Route 8: Return Book Action - **URL path**: `/return/<int:borrow_id>` - **Function name**: `return_book` - **HTTP method**: POST - **Template rendered**: Redirect back to `/my-borrows` after processing - **Context variables**: None  ### Route 9: My Reservations Page - **URL path**: `/my-reservations` - **Function name**: `my_reservations` - **HTTP method**: GET - **Template rendered**: `my_reservations.html` - **Context variables**:   - `reservations` (list of dict): Each dict contains:     - `reservation_id` (int)     - `title` (str)     - `reservation_date` (str)     - `status` (str)  ### Route 10: Cancel Reservation Action - **URL path**: `/cancel-reservation/<int:reservation_id>` - **Function name**: `cancel_reservation` - **HTTP method**: POST - **Template rendered**: Redirect back to `/my-reservations` - **Context variables**: None  ### Route 11: My Reviews Page - **URL path**: `/my-reviews` - **Function name**: `my_reviews` - **HTTP method**: GET - **Template rendered**: `my_reviews.html` - **Context variables**:   - `reviews` (list of dict): Each dict contains:     - `review_id` (int)     - `book_title` (str)     - `rating` (int)     - `review_text` (str)  ### Route 12: Edit Review Page (GET) - **URL path**: `/write-review/<int:book_id>` or `/edit-review/<int:review_id>` - **Function name**: `write_edit_review_get` - **HTTP method**: GET - **Template rendered**: `write_review.html` - **Context variables**:   - `book` (dict): with `book_id` (int) and title info   - `existing_review` (dict or None): if editing, includes `rating` (int), `review_text` (str)  ### Route 13: Submit Review (POST) - **URL path**: `/write-review/<int:book_id>` or `/edit-review/<int:review_id>` - **Function name**: `write_edit_review_post` - **HTTP method**: POST - **Template rendered**: Redirect back to book details page on success - **Context variables**: For form validation errors, re-render with error messages  ### Route 14: Delete Review Action - **URL path**: `/delete-review/<int:review_id>` - **Function name**: `delete_review` - **HTTP method**: POST - **Template rendered**: Redirect back to `/my-reviews` - **Context variables**: None  ### Route 15: User Profile Page - **URL path**: `/profile` - **Function name**: `user_profile` - **HTTP method**: GET - **Template rendered**: `profile.html` - **Context variables**:   - `username` (str)   - `email` (str)   - `borrow_history` (list of dict): List of all previously borrowed books:     - `title` (str)     - `borrow_date` (str)     - `return_date` (str or None)  ### Route 16: Update Profile (POST) - **URL path**: `/profile` - **Function name**: `update_profile` - **HTTP method**: POST - **Template rendered**: `profile.html`, possibly with success or error messages - **Context variables**: Same as GET  ### Route 17: Payment Confirmation Page - **URL path**: `/payment/<int:fine_id>` - **Function name**: `payment_confirmation_get` - **HTTP method**: GET - **Template rendered**: `payment_confirmation.html` - **Context variables**:   - `fine_amount` (float)   - `fine_id` (int)  ### Route 18: Confirm Payment (POST) - **URL path**: `/payment/<int:fine_id>` - **Function name**: `payment_confirmation_post` - **HTTP method**: POST - **Template rendered**: Redirect back to `/profile` on success - **Context variables**: None  ---  ## Section 2: HTML Template Specifications (Frontend Development)  ### Template 1: dashboard.html - **File path**: `templates/dashboard.html` - **Page Title**: "Library Dashboard" - **Elements**:   - `dashboard-page` (div)   - `welcome-message` (h1) - Displays welcome message with username   - `browse-books-button` (button) - Navigates to book catalog   - `my-borrows-button` (button) - Navigates to my borrowings - **Accessible Context Variables**:   - `username` (str) - **Navigation mappings**:   - `browse-books-button` onclick uses `url_for('book_catalog')`   - `my-borrows-button` onclick uses `url_for('my_borrowings')`  ### Template 2: catalog.html - **File path**: `templates/catalog.html` - **Page Title**: "Book Catalog" - **Elements**:   - `catalog-page` (div)   - `search-input` (input)   - `book-grid` (div) contains cards for each book with:     - Buttons with ID pattern: `view-book-button-{{ book.book_id }}` (button)   - `back-to-dashboard` (button) - **Accessible Context Variables**:   - `books` (list of dict): Each dict with keys: `book_id`, `title`, `author`, `status` - **Navigation mappings**:   - Each `view-book-button-{{ book.book_id }}` uses `url_for('book_details', book_id=book.book_id)`   - `back-to-dashboard` uses `url_for('dashboard')`  ### Template 3: book_details.html - **File path**: `templates/book_details.html` - **Page Title**: "Book Details" - **Elements**:   - `book-details-page` (div)   - `book-title` (h1)   - `book-author` (div)   - `book-status` (div)   - `borrow-button` (button)   - `reviews-section` (div) with all reviews   - `write-review-button` (button)   - `back-to-catalog` (button) - **Accessible Context Variables**:   - `book` (dict) with `book_id`, `title`, `author`, `status`   - `reviews` (list of dict): `review_id`, `username`, `rating`, `review_text`, `review_date` - **Navigation mappings**:   - `borrow-button` uses `url_for('borrow_confirmation_get', book_id=book.book_id)`   - `write-review-button` uses `url_for('write_edit_review_get', book_id=book.book_id)`   - `back-to-catalog` uses `url_for('book_catalog')`  ### Template 4: borrow_confirmation.html - **File path**: `templates/borrow_confirmation.html` - **Page Title**: "Borrow Confirmation" - **Elements**:   - `borrow-page` (div)   - `borrow-book-info` (div)   - `due-date-display` (div)   - `confirm-borrow-button` (button) - Form submission button   - `cancel-borrow-button` (button) - Navigates back - **Accessible Context Variables**:   - `book` (dict) with `book_id`, `title`   - `due_date` (str) - **Navigation mappings**:   - `confirm-borrow-button` in form POSTs to `/borrow/{{ book.book_id }}`   - `cancel-borrow-button` uses `url_for('book_details', book_id=book.book_id)`  ### Template 5: my_borrows.html - **File path**: `templates/my_borrows.html` - **Page Title**: "My Borrowings" - **Elements**:   - `my-borrows-page` (div)   - `filter-status` (dropdown)   - `borrows-table` (table) with rows containing:     - `return-book-button-{{ borrow.borrow_id }}` (button) for active borrows   - `back-to-dashboard` (button) - **Accessible Context Variables**:   - `borrowings` (list of dict): each dict with `borrow_id`, `title`, `borrow_date`, `due_date`, `status`   - `filter_status` (str) - **Navigation mappings**:   - `return-book-button-{{ borrow.borrow_id }}` form POST to `/return/{{ borrow.borrow_id }}`   - `back-to-dashboard` uses `url_for('dashboard')`  ### Template 6: my_reservations.html - **File path**: `templates/my_reservations.html` - **Page Title**: "My Reservations" - **Elements**:   - `reservations-page` (div)   - `reservations-table` (table) with rows containing:     - `cancel-reservation-button-{{ reservation.reservation_id }}` (button)   - `back-to-dashboard` (button) - **Accessible Context Variables**:   - `reservations` (list of dict): with `reservation_id`, `title`, `reservation_date`, `status` - **Navigation mappings**:   - `cancel-reservation-button-{{ reservation.reservation_id }}` form POST to `/cancel-reservation/{{ reservation.reservation_id }}`   - `back-to-dashboard` uses `url_for('dashboard')`  ### Template 7: my_reviews.html - **File path**: `templates/my_reviews.html` - **Page Title**: "My Reviews" - **Elements**:   - `reviews-page` (div)   - `reviews-list` (div) with each review including:     - `edit-review-button-{{ review.review_id }}` (button)     - `delete-review-button-{{ review.review_id }}` (button)   - `back-to-dashboard` (button) - **Accessible Context Variables**:   - `reviews` (list of dict) with `review_id`, `book_title`, `rating`, `review_text` - **Navigation mappings**:   - `edit-review-button-{{ review.review_id }}` uses `url_for('write_edit_review_get', book_id=None)` or routed via review_id   - `delete-review-button-{{ review.review_id }}` form POST to `/delete-review/{{ review.review_id }}`   - `back-to-dashboard` uses `url_for('dashboard')`  ### Template 8: write_review.html - **File path**: `templates/write_review.html` - **Page Title**: "Write Review" - **Elements**:   - `write-review-page` (div)   - `book-info-display` (div)   - `rating-input` (dropdown, 1-5 stars)   - `review-text` (textarea)   - `submit-review-button` (button)   - `back-to-book` (button) - **Accessible Context Variables**:   - `book` (dict): with `book_id`, `title`   - `existing_review` (dict or None): with `rating`, `review_text` if editing - **Navigation mappings**:   - `submit-review-button` submits form POST to `/write-review/{{ book.book_id }}` or `/edit-review/{{ existing_review.review_id }}`   - `back-to-book` uses `url_for('book_details', book_id=book.book_id)`  ### Template 9: profile.html - **File path**: `templates/profile.html` - **Page Title**: "My Profile" - **Elements**:   - `profile-page` (div)   - `profile-username` (div)   - `profile-email` (input)   - `update-profile-button` (button)   - `borrow-history` (div)   - `back-to-dashboard` (button) - **Accessible Context Variables**:   - `username` (str)   - `email` (str)   - `borrow_history` (list of dict): each with `title`, `borrow_date`, `return_date` - **Navigation mappings**:   - `update-profile-button` triggers POST to `/profile`   - `back-to-dashboard` uses `url_for('dashboard')`  ### Template 10: payment_confirmation.html - **File path**: `templates/payment_confirmation.html` - **Page Title**: "Payment Confirmation" - **Elements**:   - `payment-page` (div)   - `fine-amount-display` (div)   - `confirm-payment-button` (button)   - `back-to-profile` (button) - **Accessible Context Variables**:   - `fine_amount` (float)   - `fine_id` (int) - **Navigation mappings**:   - `confirm-payment-button` submits POST to `/payment/{{ fine_id }}`   - `back-to-profile` uses `url_for('user_profile')`  ---  ## Section 3: Data File Schemas (Backend Development)  ### 1. users.txt - **Path**: `data/users.txt` - **Fields (pipe-delimited)**:   1. `username` (str)   2. `email` (str)   3. `phone` (str)   4. `address` (str) - **Description**: Stores user account data with contact details. - **Example rows**:   ```   john_reader|john@example.com|555-1234|123 Main St   jane_doe|jane@example.com|555-5678|789 Oak St   ``` - **Notes**: No header line. All fields are strings.  ### 2. books.txt - **Path**: `data/books.txt` - **Fields (pipe-delimited)**:   1. `book_id` (int)   2. `title` (str)   3. `author` (str)   4. `isbn` (str)   5. `genre` (str)   6. `publisher` (str)   7. `year` (int)   8. `description` (str)   9. `status` (str) - Available, Borrowed, Reserved   10. `avg_rating` (float) - **Description**: Stores complete book metadata including availability. - **Example rows**:   ```   1|To Kill a Mockingbird|Harper Lee|978-0-06-112008-4|Fiction|HarperCollins|1960|A gripping tale of racial injustice and childhood innocence.|Available|4.8   2|Sapiens|Yuval Noah Harari|978-0-06-231609-7|Non-Fiction|Harper|2011|A brief history of humankind.|Borrowed|4.6   3|1984|George Orwell|978-0-452-28423-4|Fiction|Signet Classic|1949|A dystopian social science fiction novel.|Available|4.7   ``` - **Notes**: Pipe delimiter, no header. Fields must be parsed in order.  ### 3. borrowings.txt - **Path**: `data/borrowings.txt` - **Fields (pipe-delimited)**:   1. `borrow_id` (int)   2. `username` (str)   3. `book_id` (int)   4. `borrow_date` (str, ISO date `YYYY-MM-DD`)   5. `due_date` (str, ISO date)   6. `return_date` (str or empty, ISO date)   7. `status` (str) - Active, Returned, Overdue   8. `fine_amount` (float) - **Description**: Records each borrowing transaction details, status, and fines if any. - **Example rows**:   ```   1|john_reader|2|2024-11-01|2024-11-15||Active|0   2|jane_doe|1|2024-10-20|2024-11-03|2024-11-02|Returned|0   3|john_reader|3|2024-10-15|2024-10-29||Overdue|5.00   ``` - **Notes**: Empty `return_date` indicates not returned yet.  ### 4. reservations.txt - **Path**: `data/reservations.txt` - **Fields (pipe-delimited)**:   1. `reservation_id` (int)   2. `username` (str)   3. `book_id` (int)   4. `reservation_date` (str, ISO date)   5. `status` (str) - Active, Cancelled - **Description**: Tracks book reservations made by users. - **Example rows**:   ```   1|jane_doe|4|2024-11-10|Active   2|john_reader|2|2024-10-25|Cancelled   ``` - **Notes**: Status reflects current valid or cancelled reservations.  ### 5. reviews.txt - **Path**: `data/reviews.txt` - **Fields (pipe-delimited)**:   1. `review_id` (int)   2. `username` (str)   3. `book_id` (int)   4. `rating` (int, 1-5)   5. `review_text` (str)   6. `review_date` (str, ISO date) - **Description**: Stores user reviews and ratings for books. - **Example rows**:   ```   1|jane_doe|1|5|An absolute masterpiece! A must-read.|2024-11-03   2|john_reader|3|4|Thought-provoking and eerily relevant today.|2024-10-20   ``` - **Notes**: Ratings limited to integers 1 through 5.  ### 6. fines.txt - **Path**: `data/fines.txt` - **Fields (pipe-delimited)**:   1. `fine_id` (int)   2. `username` (str)   3. `borrow_id` (int)   4. `amount` (float)   5. `status` (str) - Unpaid, Paid   6. `date_issued` (str, ISO date) - **Description**: Records fines owed or paid by users for overdue borrowings. - **Example rows**:   ```   1|john_reader|3|5.00|Unpaid|2024-10-30   ``` - **Notes**: Status flags whether fine is paid or unpaid.  ---  This specification document fully supports independent backend and frontend development with precise route, template, and data schema definitions per user requirements.
  [app.py] from flask import Flask, render_template, redirect, url_for, request, flash import os from datetime import datetime, timedelta  app = Flask(__name__) app.secret_key = 'your_secret_key_here'  # Replace with a proper secret key in production  # Data file paths DATA_DIR = 'data' USERS_FILE = os.path.join(DATA_DIR, 'users.txt') BOOKS_FILE = os.path.join(DATA_DIR, 'books.txt') BORROWINGS_FILE = os.path.join(DATA_DIR, 'borrowings.txt') RESERVATIONS_FILE = os.path.join(DATA_DIR, 'reservations.txt') REVIEWS_FILE = os.path.join(DATA_DIR, 'reviews.txt') FINES_FILE = os.path.join(DATA_DIR, 'fines.txt')  # Helper functions for reading and writing pipe-delimited files  def read_users():     users = {}     if not os.path.exists(USERS_FILE):         return users     with open(USERS_FILE, 'r', encoding='utf-8') as f:         for line in f:             line = line.strip()             if not line:                 continue             parts = line.split('|')             if len(parts) != 4:                 continue             username, email, phone, address = parts             users[username] = {                 'username': username,                 'email': email,                 'phone': phone,                 'address': address             }     return users  def write_users(users):     lines = []     for user in users.values():         line = '|'.join([user['username'], user['email'], user['phone'], user['address']])         lines.append(line)     with open(USERS_FILE, 'w', encoding='utf-8') as f:         f.write('\n'.join(lines))   def read_books():     books = {}     if not os.path.exists(BOOKS_FILE):         return books     with open(BOOKS_FILE, 'r', encoding='utf-8') as f:         for line in f:             line = line.strip()             if not line:                 continue             parts = line.split('|')             if len(parts) != 10:                 continue             try:                 book_id = int(parts[0])                 title = parts[1]                 author = parts[2]                 isbn = parts[3]                 genre = parts[4]                 publisher = parts[5]                 year = int(parts[6])                 description = parts[7]                 status = parts[8]                 avg_rating = float(parts[9])                 books[book_id] = {                     'book_id': book_id,                     'title': title,                     'author': author,                     'isbn': isbn,                     'genre': genre,                     'publisher': publisher,                     'year': year,                     'description': description,                     'status': status,                     'avg_rating': avg_rating                 }             except ValueError:                 continue     return books   def write_books(books):     lines = []     for book in books.values():         line = '|'.join([             str(book['book_id']),             book['title'],             book['author'],             book['isbn'],             book['genre'],             book['publisher'],             str(book['year']),             book['description'],             book['status'],             f"{book['avg_rating']:.1f}"         ])         lines.append(line)     with open(BOOKS_FILE, 'w', encoding='utf-8') as f:         f.write('\n'.join(lines))   def read_borrowings():     borrowings = {}     if not os.path.exists(BORROWINGS_FILE):         return borrowings     with open(BORROWINGS_FILE, 'r', encoding='utf-8') as f:         for line in f:             line = line.strip()             if not line:                 continue             parts = line.split('|')             if len(parts) != 8:                 continue             try:                 borrow_id = int(parts[0])                 username = parts[1]                 book_id = int(parts[2])                 borrow_date = parts[3]                 due_date = parts[4]                 return_date = parts[5] if parts[5] else None                 status = parts[6]                 fine_amount = float(parts[7])                 borrowings[borrow_id] = {                     'borrow_id': borrow_id,                     'username': username,                     'book_id': book_id,                     'borrow_date': borrow_date,                     'due_date': due_date,                     'return_date': return_date,                     'status': status,                     'fine_amount': fine_amount                 }             except ValueError:                 continue     return borrowings   def write_borrowings(borrowings):     lines = []     for borrow in borrowings.values():         return_date_str = borrow['return_date'] if borrow['return_date'] else ''         line = '|'.join([             str(borrow['borrow_id']),             borrow['username'],             str(borrow['book_id']),             borrow['borrow_date'],             borrow['due_date'],             return_date_str,             borrow['status'],             f"{borrow['fine_amount']:.2f}"         ])         lines.append(line)     with open(BORROWINGS_FILE, 'w', encoding='utf-8') as f:         f.write('\n'.join(lines))   def read_reservations():     reservations = {}     if not os.path.exists(RESERVATIONS_FILE):         return reservations     with open(RESERVATIONS_FILE, 'r', encoding='utf-8') as f:         for line in f:             line = line.strip()             if not line:                 continue             parts = line.split('|')             if len(parts) != 5:                 continue             try:                 reservation_id = int(parts[0])                 username = parts[1]                 book_id = int(parts[2])                 reservation_date = parts[3]                 status = parts[4]                 reservations[reservation_id] = {                     'reservation_id': reservation_id,                     'username': username,                     'book_id': book_id,                     'reservation_date': reservation_date,                     'status': status                 }             except ValueError:                 continue     return reservations   def write_reservations(reservations):     lines = []     for res in reservations.values():         line = '|'.join([             str(res['reservation_id']),             res['username'],             str(res['book_id']),             res['reservation_date'],             res['status']         ])         lines.append(line)     with open(RESERVATIONS_FILE, 'w', encoding='utf-8') as f:         f.write('\n'.join(lines))   def read_reviews():     reviews = {}     if not os.path.exists(REVIEWS_FILE):         return reviews     with open(REVIEWS_FILE, 'r', encoding='utf-8') as f:         for line in f:             line = line.strip()             if not line:                 continue             parts = line.split('|')             if len(parts) != 6:                 continue             try:                 review_id = int(parts[0])                 username = parts[1]                 book_id = int(parts[2])                 rating = int(parts[3])                 review_text = parts[4]                 review_date = parts[5]                 reviews[review_id] = {                     'review_id': review_id,                     'username': username,                     'book_id': book_id,                     'rating': rating,                     'review_text': review_text,                     'review_date': review_date                 }             except ValueError:                 continue     return reviews   def write_reviews(reviews):     lines = []     for rev in reviews.values():         line = '|'.join([             str(rev['review_id']),             rev['username'],             str(rev['book_id']),             str(rev['rating']),             rev['review_text'],             rev['review_date']         ])         lines.append(line)     with open(REVIEWS_FILE, 'w', encoding='utf-8') as f:         f.write('\n'.join(lines))   def read_fines():     fines = {}     if not os.path.exists(FINES_FILE):         return fines     with open(FINES_FILE, 'r', encoding='utf-8') as f:         for line in f:             line = line.strip()             if not line:                 continue             parts = line.split('|')             if len(parts) != 6:                 continue             try:                 fine_id = int(parts[0])                 username = parts[1]                 borrow_id = int(parts[2])                 amount = float(parts[3])                 status = parts[4]                 date_issued = parts[5]                 fines[fine_id] = {                     'fine_id': fine_id,                     'username': username,                     'borrow_id': borrow_id,                     'amount': amount,                     'status': status,                     'date_issued': date_issued                 }             except ValueError:                 continue     return fines   def write_fines(fines):     lines = []     for fine in fines.values():         line = '|'.join([             str(fine['fine_id']),             fine['username'],             str(fine['borrow_id']),             f"{fine['amount']:.2f}",             fine['status'],             fine['date_issued']         ])         lines.append(line)     with open(FINES_FILE, 'w', encoding='utf-8') as f:         f.write('\n'.join(lines))   # ------------------------------------------------------------------ # For simplicity, simulate a logged-in user # In real application, use proper authentication and sessions LOGGED_IN_USERNAME = 'john_reader'  # Utility for date handling  def get_current_date():     return datetime.utcnow().date()  def date_to_str(date_obj):     return date_obj.strftime('%Y-%m-%d')  def str_to_date(date_str):     try:         return datetime.strptime(date_str, '%Y-%m-%d').date()     except:         return None  # Calculate fine based on days overdue # Let's say fine is $1 per day overdue  def calculate_fine(due_date_str, return_date_str=None):     due_date = str_to_date(due_date_str)     if due_date is None:         return 0.0     if return_date_str:         return_date = str_to_date(return_date_str)         if return_date is None:             return 0.0     else:         return_date = get_current_date()      delta = (return_date - due_date).days     if delta > 0:         return float(delta)  # $1 per day     return 0.0  # Update borrowings status and fines  def refresh_borrowings_status():     borrowings = read_borrowings()     fines = read_fines()     fine_updated = False     for borrow in borrowings.values():         if borrow['status'] == 'Active':             due_date_obj = str_to_date(borrow['due_date'])             today = get_current_date()             if due_date_obj and today > due_date_obj:                 borrow['status'] = 'Overdue'                 # Calculate or update fine                 fine_amount = calculate_fine(borrow['due_date'])                 borrow['fine_amount'] = fine_amount                  # Add fine record if not exist or update                 fine_found = False                 for fine in fines.values():                     if fine['borrow_id'] == borrow['borrow_id'] and fine['username'] == borrow['username'] and fine['status'] == 'Unpaid':                         if abs(fine['amount'] - fine_amount) > 0.01:                             fine['amount'] = fine_amount                             fine_updated = True                         fine_found = True                         break                 if not fine_found and fine_amount > 0:                     new_fine_id = max(fines.keys()) + 1 if fines else 1                     fine = {                         'fine_id': new_fine_id,                         'username': borrow['username'],                         'borrow_id': borrow['borrow_id'],                         'amount': fine_amount,                         'status': 'Unpaid',                         'date_issued': date_to_str(today)                     }                     fines[new_fine_id] = fine                     fine_updated = True             else:                 # If before due date, no fine                 borrow['fine_amount'] = 0.0      if fine_updated:         write_fines(fines)     write_borrowings(borrowings)   # Route 1: Root redirect @app.route('/') def root_redirect():     return redirect(url_for('dashboard'))  # Route 2: Dashboard @app.route('/dashboard') def dashboard():     username = LOGGED_IN_USERNAME     # Confirm user exists     users = read_users()     if username not in users:         flash('User not found.', 'error')         return redirect(url_for('root_redirect'))     return render_template('dashboard.html', username=username)  # Route 3: Book Catalog @app.route('/catalog') def book_catalog():     books = list(read_books().values())     # We can pass all, frontend can handle display     # Just send book_id, title, author, status     books_for_template = [{         'book_id': b['book_id'],         'title': b['title'],         'author': b['author'],         'status': b['status']     } for b in books]     return render_template('catalog.html', books=books_for_template)  # Route 4: Book Details @app.route('/book/<int:book_id>') def book_details(book_id):     books = read_books()     if book_id not in books:         flash('Book not found.', 'error')         return redirect(url_for('book_catalog'))     book = books[book_id]      reviews = read_reviews().values()     book_reviews = []     for r in reviews:         if r['book_id'] == book_id:             book_reviews.append({                 'review_id': r['review_id'],                 'username': r['username'],                 'rating': r['rating'],                 'review_text': r['review_text'],                 'review_date': r['review_date']             })     # Sort reviews by review_date descending     book_reviews.sort(key=lambda x: x['review_date'], reverse=True)      return render_template('book_details.html', book=book, reviews=book_reviews)  # Route 5 (GET): Borrow Confirmation GET @app.route('/borrow/<int:book_id>', methods=['GET']) def borrow_confirmation_get(book_id):     books = read_books()     if book_id not in books:         flash('Book not found.', 'error')         return redirect(url_for('book_catalog'))     book = books[book_id]     due_date_obj = get_current_date() + timedelta(days=14)     due_date_str = date_to_str(due_date_obj)     book_brief = {         'book_id': book['book_id'],         'title': book['title']     }     return render_template('borrow_confirmation.html', book=book_brief, due_date=due_date_str)  # Route 6 (POST): Borrow Confirmation POST @app.route('/borrow/<int:book_id>', methods=['POST']) def borrow_confirmation_post(book_id):     books = read_books()     users = read_users()     borrowings = read_borrowings()      if LOGGED_IN_USERNAME not in users:         flash('User not found.', 'error')         return redirect(url_for('dashboard'))      if book_id not in books:         flash('Book not found.', 'error')         return redirect(url_for('book_catalog'))      book = books[book_id]      # Check book availability     if book['status'] != 'Available':         flash('Book is not available for borrowing.', 'error')         due_date_obj = get_current_date() + timedelta(days=14)         due_date_str = date_to_str(due_date_obj)         book_brief = {'book_id': book['book_id'], 'title': book['title']}         return render_template('borrow_confirmation.html', book=book_brief, due_date=due_date_str)      # Check if user already has active or overdue borrow for this book     for b in borrowings.values():         if (b['username'] == LOGGED_IN_USERNAME and b['book_id'] == book_id and              b['status'] in ('Active', 'Overdue')):             flash('You have already borrowed this book and it is not yet returned.', 'error')             due_date_obj = get_current_date() + timedelta(days=14)             due_date_str = date_to_str(due_date_obj)             book_brief = {'book_id': book['book_id'], 'title': book['title']}             return render_template('borrow_confirmation.html', book=book_brief, due_date=due_date_str)      # Proceed with borrowing     new_borrow_id = max(borrowings.keys()) + 1 if borrowings else 1     borrow_date_str = date_to_str(get_current_date())     due_date_obj = get_current_date() + timedelta(days=14)     due_date_str = date_to_str(due_date_obj)      new_borrow = {         'borrow_id': new_borrow_id,         'username': LOGGED_IN_USERNAME,         'book_id': book_id,         'borrow_date': borrow_date_str,         'due_date': due_date_str,         'return_date': None,         'status': 'Active',         'fine_amount': 0.0     }      borrowings[new_borrow_id] = new_borrow      # Update book status to Borrowed     book['status'] = 'Borrowed'     books[book_id] = book      # Write updated data     write_borrowings(borrowings)     write_books(books)      flash('Book borrowed successfully.', 'success')     return redirect(url_for('my_borrowings'))  # Route 7: My Borrowings @app.route('/my-borrows') def my_borrowings():     refresh_borrowings_status()  # Update statuses and fines     borrowings = read_borrowings()     books = read_books()      # We only show borrowings for current user     user_borrowings = [b for b in borrowings.values() if b['username'] == LOGGED_IN_USERNAME]      filter_status = request.args.get('filter_status', '')     if filter_status:         filter_status = filter_status.capitalize()         user_borrowings = [b for b in user_borrowings if b['status'] == filter_status]      borrow_list = []     for b in user_borrowings:         book = books.get(b['book_id'])         title = book['title'] if book else 'Unknown'         borrow_list.append({             'borrow_id': b['borrow_id'],             'title': title,             'borrow_date': b['borrow_date'],             'due_date': b['due_date'],             'status': b['status']         })      return render_template('my_borrows.html', borrowings=borrow_list, filter_status=filter_status)  # Route 8: Return book @app.route('/return/<int:borrow_id>', methods=['POST']) def return_book(borrow_id):     borrowings = read_borrowings()     books = read_books()      if borrow_id not in borrowings:         flash('Borrowing record not found.', 'error')         return redirect(url_for('my_borrowings'))      borrow = borrowings[borrow_id]     if borrow['username'] != LOGGED_IN_USERNAME:         flash('You cannot return a borrow that is not yours.', 'error')         return redirect(url_for('my_borrowings'))      if borrow['status'] == 'Returned':         flash('This book has already been returned.', 'info')         return redirect(url_for('my_borrowings'))      # Update borrow return date and status     return_date_str = date_to_str(get_current_date())     borrow['return_date'] = return_date_str      # Update status depending on if overdue or not     due_date_obj = str_to_date(borrow['due_date'])     return_date_obj = str_to_date(return_date_str)      if return_date_obj and due_date_obj and return_date_obj > due_date_obj:         borrow['status'] = 'Returned'  # Returned but was overdue     else:         borrow['status'] = 'Returned'      # Update book status to Available     book_id = borrow['book_id']     book = books.get(book_id)     if book:         # Check if there are active reservations for this book         reservations = read_reservations()         has_active_reservation = any(             r['book_id'] == book_id and r['status'] == 'Active' for r in reservations.values())         if has_active_reservation:             book['status'] = 'Reserved'         else:             book['status'] = 'Available'          books[book_id] = book      # Clear fine_amount for borrowing if returned     borrow['fine_amount'] = 0.0      # Write updates     borrowings[borrow_id] = borrow     write_borrowings(borrowings)     write_books(books)      flash('Book returned successfully.', 'success')     return redirect(url_for('my_borrowings'))  # Route 9: My Reservations @app.route('/my-reservations') def my_reservations():     reservations = read_reservations()     books = read_books()      user_reservations = [r for r in reservations.values() if r['username'] == LOGGED_IN_USERNAME]     reservations_list = []     for r in user_reservations:         book = books.get(r['book_id'])         title = book['title'] if book else 'Unknown'         reservations_list.append({             'reservation_id': r['reservation_id'],             'title': title,             'reservation_date': r['reservation_date'],             'status': r['status']         })      return render_template('my_reservations.html', reservations=reservations_list)  # Route 10: Cancel Reservation @app.route('/cancel-reservation/<int:reservation_id>', methods=['POST']) def cancel_reservation(reservation_id):     reservations = read_reservations()      if reservation_id not in reservations:         flash('Reservation not found.', 'error')         return redirect(url_for('my_reservations'))      reservation = reservations[reservation_id]      if reservation['username'] != LOGGED_IN_USERNAME:         flash('You can only cancel your own reservations.', 'error')         return redirect(url_for('my_reservations'))      if reservation['status'] == 'Cancelled':         flash('Reservation already cancelled.', 'info')         return redirect(url_for('my_reservations'))      # Cancel the reservation     reservation['status'] = 'Cancelled'     reservations[reservation_id] = reservation     write_reservations(reservations)      # Update book status if needed     books = read_books()     book = books.get(reservation['book_id'])     if book:         # Check if there are any other active reservations         has_other_active = any(             r for r in
