=== LLM_call: FrontendDeveloper ===
INPUT: === test_report.md ===
# Test Report: OnlineLibrary Application

## Summary
Comprehensive functional, integration, and regression testing was conducted against the OnlineLibrary Flask application covering all main user flows, UI element verification, and data consistency checks as per design_spec.md. The tests were executed in a controlled environment with prepared sample data.

## Test Cases and Results

| Test Case | Description | Expected Result | Actual Result | Status |
|-----------|-------------|-----------------|---------------|--------|
| Root Redirect | Access root URL `/` should redirect to `/dashboard` | Redirect response to `/dashboard` | Redirect response (`302 FOUND`) | Passed |
| Dashboard Render | Access `/dashboard` renders dashboard page with username display | Page rendered with username present | Page rendered and username present | Passed |
| Catalog Display | Access `/catalog` lists all available books | Catalog lists all sample books | All sample books listed | Passed |
| Book Details Page | Access `/book/1` displays book details | Book details page includes book title | Book details page includes title | Passed |
| Borrow Confirmation GET | Access `/borrow/1` GET shows borrow confirmation page | Page displays selected book info and due date | Page displays correct info | Passed |
| Borrow Confirmation POST | POST `/borrow/1` borrows book if available | Book status updated, borrow record created, redirect to My Borrows | Book status is 'Borrowed', borrow created, redirect occurs | Passed |
| My Borrowings Page | Access `/my-borrows` lists current user borrowings | Borrow list includes borrowed book | Borrow list includes borrowed book | Passed |
| Return Book POST | POST return for active borrow marks as returned and updates book status | Borrow status updated to 'Returned', book status 'Available' | Borrow marked returned, book status available | Passed |
| Write Review GET | Access write review page for book without existing review | Page displays review form for book | Review form displayed with correct book info | Passed |
| Submit Review POST | Submit new review for a book | Review added, average rating updated | Review created and data updated | Passed |
| Edit Review GET | Access edit review page for user's review | Page displays existing review data for editing | Correct review loaded in form | Passed |
| Delete Review POST | Delete existing review | Review is removed from data | Review deleted successfully | Passed |
| User Profile GET | Access user profile page | Profile page shows user info and borrow history | Profile page rendered with correct user info | Passed |
| Update Profile POST | Update user profile with valid details | User data updated in users file | User data updated correctly | Passed |
| My Reservations GET | Access user reservations page | Page loads with current reservations (empty initially) | Page loaded successfully | Passed |
| Cancel Reservation POST Nonexistent | Cancel a non-existent reservation | Redirect or error message | Failed: did not redirect or show error as expected | Failed |
| Payment Confirmation GET Nonexistent Fine | Access payment page for non-existent fine | Redirect or error message | Failed: no redirect or error detected | Failed |
| Payment Confirmation POST Nonexistent Fine | Submit payment for non-existent fine | Redirect or error message | Failed: no redirect or error detected | Failed |

## UI Element Verification
All templates contain required element IDs and navigation mappings as per design specification. Dynamic IDs with Jinja2 variables are implemented correctly.

## Data Integrity and Persistence
CRUD operations on borrowings, reviews, reservations, and user profile update correctly modify respective data files with proper field formats and no corruption.

## Issues Found
- Cancel Reservation POST on nonexistent reservation ID does not redirect or flash error as expected.
- Payment Confirmation GET and POST for nonexistent fine similarly do not redirect or notify user about missing records.

## Recommendations
- Improve error handling and user feedback for operations on non-existent reservation or fine IDs.

## Overall Conclusion
The OnlineLibrary system is functionally robust with correct data handling and UI consistency for all main flows. Minor improvements are recommended on error handling paths.

---

**Test Status:** NEED_MODIFY (due to failed error handling cases in reservation cancellation and payment confirmation on invalid IDs)


=== /home/jun/0624/emnlp_CaMP/results/Software-web-ablation/20260712_000000_io_chaos_seed1_wo_failover/OnlineLibrary/io_chaos/templates/book_details.html ===
<!DOCTYPE html>
<html lang="en">
<head>
    <meta charset="UTF-8">
    <title>Book Details</title>
</head>
<body>
    <div id="book-details-page">
        <h1 id="book-title">{{ book.title }}</h1>
        <div id="book-author">Author: {{ book.author }}</div>
        <div id="book-status">Status: {{ book.status }}</div>
        <button id="borrow-button" onclick="window.location.href='{{ url_for('borrow_confirmation_get', book_id=book.book_id) }}'">Borrow</button>
        <div id="reviews-section">
            <h2>Reviews</h2>
            {% if reviews %}
            <ul>
                {% for review in reviews %}
                <li>
                    <strong>{{ review.username }}</strong> ({{ review.review_date }}): 
                    Rating: {{ review.rating }} / 5
                    <p>{{ review.review_text }}</p>
                </li>
                {% endfor %}
            </ul>
            {% else %}
            <p>No reviews yet.</p>
            {% endif %}
        </div>
        <button id="write-review-button" onclick="window.location.href='{{ url_for('write_edit_review_get', book_id=book.book_id) }}'">Write Review</button>
        <button id="back-to-catalog" onclick="window.location.href='{{ url_for('book_catalog') }}'">Back to Catalog</button>
    </div>
</body>
</html>


=== /home/jun/0624/emnlp_CaMP/results/Software-web-ablation/20260712_000000_io_chaos_seed1_wo_failover/OnlineLibrary/io_chaos/templates/borrow_confirmation.html ===
<!DOCTYPE html>
<html lang="en">
<head>
    <meta charset="UTF-8">
    <title>Borrow Confirmation</title>
</head>
<body>
    <div id="borrow-page">
        <div id="borrow-book-info">
            <h2>Confirm borrowing:</h2>
            <p>Book: {{ book.title }}</p>
        </div>
        <div id="due-date-display">
            <p>Due Date: {{ due_date }}</p>
        </div>
        <form method="POST" action="{{ url_for('borrow_confirmation_post', book_id=book.book_id) }}">
            <button id="confirm-borrow-button" type="submit">Confirm Borrow</button>
        </form>
        <button id="cancel-borrow-button" onclick="window.location.href='{{ url_for('book_details', book_id=book.book_id) }}'">Cancel</button>
    </div>
</body>
</html>

<!DOCTYPE html>
<html lang="en">
<head>
    <meta charset="UTF-8">
    <title>Book Catalog</title>
</head>
<body>
    <div id="catalog-page">
        <input id="search-input" type="text" placeholder="Search books...">
        <div id="book-grid">
            {% for book in books %}
            <div class="book-card">
                <h3>{{ book.title }}</h3>
                <p>Author: {{ book.author }}</p>
                <p>Status: {{ book.status }}</p>
                <button id="view-book-button-{{ book.book_id }}" onclick="window.location.href='{{ url_for('book_details', book_id=book.book_id) }}'">View Details</button>
            </div>
            {% endfor %}
        </div>
        <button id="back-to-dashboard" onclick="window.location.href='{{ url_for('dashboard') }}'">Back to Dashboard</button>
    </div>
</body>
</html>

=== /home/jun/0624/emnlp_CaMP/results/Software-web-ablation/20260712_000000_io_chaos_seed1_wo_failover/OnlineLibrary/io_chaos/templates/my_borrows.html ===
<!DOCTYPE html>
<html lang="en">
<head>
    <meta charset="UTF-8">
    <title>My Borrowings</title>
</head>
<body>
    <div id="my-borrows-page">
        <label for="filter-status">Filter Status:</label>
        <select id="filter-status" name="filter-status" onchange="location = '?filter_status=' + this.value;">
            <option value="" {% if filter_status == '' %}selected{% endif %}>All</option>
            <option value="Active" {% if filter_status == 'Active' %}selected{% endif %}>Active</option>
            <option value="Returned" {% if filter_status == 'Returned' %}selected{% endif %}>Returned</option>
            <option value="Overdue" {% if filter_status == 'Overdue' %}selected{% endif %}>Overdue</option>
        </select>
        <table id="borrows-table" border="1">
            <thead>
                <tr>
                    <th>Title</th>
                    <th>Borrow Date</th>
                    <th>Due Date</th>
                    <th>Status</th>
                    <th>Action</th>
                </tr>
            </thead>
            <tbody>
                {% for borrow in borrowings %}
                <tr>
                    <td>{{ borrow.title }}</td>
                    <td>{{ borrow.borrow_date }}</td>
                    <td>{{ borrow.due_date }}</td>
                    <td>{{ borrow.status }}</td>
                    <td>
                    {% if borrow.status == 'Active' %}
                        <form method="POST" action="{{ url_for('return_book', borrow_id=borrow.borrow_id) }}">
                            <button id="return-book-button-{{ borrow.borrow_id }}" type="submit">Return Book</button>
                        </form>
                    {% else %}
                        N/A
                    {% endif %}
                    </td>
                </tr>
                {% endfor %}
            </tbody>
        </table>
        <button id="back-to-dashboard" onclick="window.location.href='{{ url_for('dashboard') }}'">Back to Dashboard</button>
    </div>
</body>
</html>

datetime.utcnow().date()  def date_to_str(date_obj):     return date_obj.strftime('%Y-%m-%d')  def str_to_date(date_str):     try:         return datetime.strptime(date_str, '%Y-%m-%d').date()     except:         return None  # Calculate fine based on days overdue # Let's say fine is $1 per day overdue  def calculate_fine(due_date_str, return_date_str=None):     due_date = str_to_date(due_date_str)     if due_date is None:         return 0.0     if return_date_str:         return_date = str_to_date(return_date_str)         if return_date is None:             return 0.0     else:         return_date = get_current_date()      delta = (return_date - due_date).days     if delta > 0:         return float(delta)  # $1 per day     return 0.0  # Update borrowings status and fines  def refresh_borrowings_status():     borrowings = read_borrowings()     fines = read_fines()     fine_updated = False     for borrow in borrowings.values():         if borrow['status'] == 'Active':             due_date_obj = str_to_date(borrow['due_date'])             today = get_current_date()             if due_date_obj and today > due_date_obj:                 borrow['status'] = 'Overdue'                 # Calculate or update fine                 fine_amount = calculate_fine(borrow['due_date'])                 borrow['fine_amount'] = fine_amount                  # Add fine record if not exist or update                 fine_found = False                 for fine in fines.values():                     if fine['borrow_id'] == borrow['borrow_id'] and fine['username'] == borrow['username'] and fine['status'] == 'Unpaid':                         if abs(fine['amount'] - fine_amount) > 0.01:                             fine['amount'] = fine_amount                             fine_updated = True                         fine_found = True                         break                 if not fine_found and fine_amount > 0:                     new_fine_id = max(fines.keys()) + 1 if fines else 1                     fine = {                         'fine_id': new_fine_id,                         'username': borrow['username'],                         'borrow_id': borrow['borrow_id'],                         'amount': fine_amount,                         'status': 'Unpaid',                         'date_issued': date_to_str(today)                     }                     fines[new_fine_id] = fine                     fine_updated = True             else:                 # If before due date, no fine                 borrow['fine_amount'] = 0.0              'review_date': r['review_date']             })     # Sort reviews by review_date descending     book_reviews.sort(key=lambda x: x['review_date'], reverse=True)      return methods=['POST']) def borrow_confirmation_post(book_id):     books = read_books()     users = read_users()     borrowings = read_borrowings()      if LOGGED_IN_USERNAME not in users:         flash('User not found.', 'error')         return redirect(url_for('dashboard'))      if book_id not in books:         flash('Book not found.', 'error')         return redirect(url_for('book_catalog'))      book = books[book_id]      # Check book availability     if book['status'] != 'Available':         flash('Book is not available for borrowing.', 'error')         due_date_obj = get_current_date() render_template('borrow_confirmation.html', book=book_brief, due_date=due_date_str)      # Check if user already has active or overdue borrow for this book     for b in borrowings.values():         if returned.', 'error')             due_date_obj = get_current_date() + timedelta(days=14)             due_date_str = date_to_str(due_date_obj)             book_brief = {'book_id': book['book_id'], borrowings = read_borrowings()     books = read_books()      if borrow_id not in borrowings:         flash('Borrowing record not found.', 'error')         return redirect(url_for('my_borrowings'))      borrow = borrowings[borrow_id]     if borrow['username'] != LOGGED_IN_USERNAME:         flash('You cannot return a borrow that is not yours.', 'error')         return redirect(url_for('my_borrowings'))      if borrow['status'] == 'Returned':         flash('This book has already been returned.', 'info')         return redirect(url_for('my_borrowings'))      # Update str_to_date(borrow['due_date'])     return_date_obj = str_to_date(return_date_str)      if return_date_obj and due_date_obj and return_date_obj > due_date_obj:         borrow['status'] = 'Returned'  # Returned but was overdue     else:         borrow['status'] = 'Returned'      # Update book status to Available     book_id = borrow['book_id']     book = books.get(book_id)     if book:         # Check if there are active reservations for this book         reservations = read_reservations()         has_active_reservation = any(             r['book_id'] == book_id and r['status'] == 'Active' for r in reservations.values())         if has_active_reservation:             book['status'] = 'Reserved'         else:             book['status'] = 'Available'          books[book_id] = book      # Clear fine_amount for borrowing if returned     borrow['fine_amount'] = 0.0      # Write updates     borrowings[borrow_id] = borrow     write_borrowings(borrowings)     write_books(books)      flash('Book returned successfully.', 'success')     return redirect(url_for('my_borrowings'))  # Route 9: My Reservations @app.route('/my-reservations') def my_reservations():     reservations = read_reservations()     books = read_books()      user_reservations = [r for r in reservations.values() if r['username'] == LOGGED_IN_USERNAME]     reservations_list = []     for r in user_reservations:         book = books.get(r['book_id'])         title = book['title'] if book else 'Unknown'         reservations_list.append({             'reservation_id': r['reservation_id'],             'title': title,             'reservation_date': r['reservation_date'],             'status': r['status']         })      return render_template('my_reservations.html', reservations=reservations_list)  # Route 10: Cancel Reservation @app.route('/cancel-reservation/<int:reservation_id>', methods=['POST']) def cancel_reservation(reservation_id):     reservations = read_reservations()      if reservation_id not in reservations:         flash('Reservation not found.', 'error')         return redirect(url_for('my_reservations'))      reservation = reservations[reservation_id]      if reservation['username'] != LOGGED_IN_USERNAME:         flash('You can only cancel your own reservations.', 'error')         return redirect(url_for('my_reservations'))      if reservation['status'] == 'Cancelled':         flash('Reservation already cancelled.', 'info')         return redirect(url_for('my_reservations'))      # Cancel the reservation     reservation['status'] = 'Cancelled'     reservations[reservation_id] = reservation     write_reservations(reservations)      # Update book status if needed     books = read_books()     book = books.get(reservation['book_id'])     if book:         # Check if there are any other active reservations         has_other_active = any(             r for r in reservations.values()             if r['book_id'] == book['book_id'] and r['status'] == 'Active'         )         if not has_other_active and book['status'] == 'Reserved':             book['status'] = 'Available'             books[book['book_id']] = book             write_books(books)      flash('Reservation cancelled successfully.', 'success')     return redirect(url_for('my_reservations'))  # Route 11: My Reviews @app.route('/my-reviews') def my_reviews():     reviews = read_reviews()     books = read_books()      user_reviews = [r for r in reviews.values() if r['username'] ==      'review_id': r['review_id'],             'book_title': book_title,             'rating': r['rating'],             'review_text': r['review_text']         })      return render_template('my_reviews.html', reviews=review_list)  # Route 12: Write/Edit Review (GET) @app.route('/write-review/<int:book_id>', methods=['GET']) @app.route('/edit-review/<int:review_id>', methods=['GET']) def write_edit_review_get(book_id=None, review_id=None):     books = read_books()     reviews = read_reviews()      if book_id is not None:         # Writing new review for book_id         if book_id not in books:             flash('Book not found.', 'error')             return redirect(url_for('book_catalog'))         book = books[book_id]         # Check if user already has a review for this book         existing_review = None         for r in reviews.values():             if r['book_id'] == book_id and r['username'] == LOGGED_IN_USERNAME:                 existing_review = r                 break         # If existing review found, redirect to edit review         if existing_review:             return redirect(url_for('write_edit_review_get', review_id=existing_review['review_id']))          book_brief = {'book_id': book['book_id'], 'title': book['title']}         return render_template('write_review.html', book=book_brief, 'rating': review['rating'],             'review_text': review['review_text']         }         return render_template('write_review.html', book=book_brief, existing_review=existing_review)      # If neither, redirect to dashboard     return redirect(url_for('dashboard'))  # Route 13: Write/Edit Review (POST) @app.route('/write-review/<int:book_id>', methods=['POST']) @app.route('/edit-review/<int:review_id>', methods=['POST']) def write_edit_review_post(book_id=None, review_id=None):     rating = request.form.get('rating')     review_text = request.form.get('review_text')      # Validation     error = None     try:         rating_int = int(rating)         if rating_int < 1 or rating_int > 5:             error = 'Rating must be between 1 and 5.'     except:         error = 'Invalid rating value.'      if not review_text or review_text.strip() == '':         error = 'Review text cannot be empty.'      books = read_books()     reviews = read_reviews()      if book_id is not None:         # Creating new review         if book_id not in books:             flash('Book not found.', 'error')             return redirect(url_for('book_catalog'))          # Check if user already has review         existing = None         for r in reviews.values():             if r['book_id'] == book_id and r['username'] == LOGGED_IN_USERNAME:                 existing = r                 break         if existing:             flash('Review already exists. Please edit it instead.', 'error')             return redirect(url_for('write_edit_review_get', review_id=existing['review_id']))          if error:             book_brief = {'book_id': book_id, 'title': books[book_id]['title']}             return render_template('write_review.html', book=book_brief, existing_review=None, error=error)          # Add new review         new_review_id = max(reviews.keys()) + 1 if reviews else 1         today_str = date_to_str(get_current_date())          new_review = {             'review_id': new_review_id,             'username': LOGGED_IN_USERNAME,             'book_id': book_id,             'rating': rating_int,             'review_text': review_text.strip(),             'review_date': today_str         }         reviews[new_review_id] = new_review          # Update average rating of book         update_book_avg_rating(book_id, reviews, books)          write_reviews(reviews)         write_books(books)          flash('Review added successfully.', 'success')         return redirect(url_for('book_details', book_id=book_id))      if review_id is not None:         # Editing existing review         if review_id not in reviews:             flash('Review not found.', 'error')             return redirect(url_for('my_reviews'))         review = reviews[review_id]          if review['username'] != LOGGED_IN_USERNAME:             flash('You can only edit your own reviews.', 'error')             return redirect(url_for('my_reviews'))          if error:             book = books.get(review['book_id'])             if not book:                 flash('Book not found.', 'error')                 return redirect(url_for('my_reviews'))             book_brief = {'book_id': book['book_id'], 'title': book['title']}             existing_review = {'rating': rating_int if not error else None, 'review_text': review_text}             return render_template('write_review.html', book=book_brief, existing_review=existing_review, error=error)          # Update data         review['rating'] = rating_int         review['review_text'] = review_text.strip()         review['review_date'] = date_to_str(get_current_date())          # Update average rating         update_book_avg_rating(review['book_id'], reviews, books)          reviews[review_id] = review         write_reviews(reviews)         write_books(books)          flash('Review updated successfully.', 'success')         return redirect(url_for('book_details', book_id=review['book_id']))      return redirect(url_for('dashboard'))  # Utility to update book average rating  def update_book_avg_rating(book_id, reviews, books):     ratings = [r['rating'] for r in reviews.values() if r['book_id'] == book_id]     if ratings:         avg = sum(ratings) / len(ratings)     else:         avg = 0.0     book = books.get(book_id)     if book:         book['avg_rating'] = round(avg, 1)  # Route 14: Delete Review @app.route('/delete-review/<int:review_id>', methods=['POST']) def delete_review(review_id):     reviews = read_reviews()      if review_id not in reviews:         flash('Review not found.', 'error')         return redirect(url_for('my_reviews'))      review = reviews[review_id]     if review['username'] != LOGGED_IN_USERNAME:         flash('You can only delete your own reviews.', 'error')         return redirect(url_for('my_reviews'))      book_id = review['book_id']     del reviews[review_id]      # After delete, update average rating     books = read_books()     update_book_avg_rating(book_id, reviews, books)      write_reviews(reviews)     write_books(books)      flash('Review deleted successfully.', 'success')     return redirect(url_for('my_reviews'))  # Route 15: User Profile (GET) @app.route('/profile', methods=['GET']) def user_profile():     users = read_users()     borrowings = read_borrowings()     books = read_books()      if LOGGED_IN_USERNAME not in users:         flash('User not found.', 'error')         return redirect(url_for('dashboard'))      user = users[LOGGED_IN_USERNAME]     # lines.append(line)\n    with open(USERS_FILE, 'w', encoding='utf-8') as f:\n        f.write('\\n'.join(lines))\n\n\ndef read_books():\n    books = {}\n    if not os.path.exists(BOOKS_FILE):\n        return books\n    with open(BOOKS_FILE, 'r', encoding='utf-8') as f:\n        for line in f:\n            line = line.strip()\n            if not line:\n                continue\n            parts = line.split('|')\n            if len(parts) != 10:\n                continue\n            try:\n                book_id = int(parts[0])\n                title = parts[1]\n                author = parts[2]\n                isbn = parts[3]\n                genre = parts[4]\n                publisher = parts[5]\n                year = int(parts[6])\n                description = parts[7]\n                status = parts[8]\n                avg_rating = float(parts[9])\n                books[book_id] = {\n                    'book_id': book_id,\n                    'title': title,\n                    'author': author,\n                    'isbn': isbn,\n                    'genre': genre,\n                    'publisher': publisher,\n                    'year': year,\n                    'description': description,\n                    'status': status,\n                    'avg_rating': avg_rating\n                }\n            except ValueError:\n                continue\n    return books\n\n\ndef write_books(books):\n    lines = []\n    for book in books.values():\n        line = '|'.join([\n            str(book['book_id']),\n            book['title'],\n            book['author'],\n            book['isbn'],\n            book['genre'],\n            book['publisher'],\n            str(book['year']),\n            book['description'],\n            book['status'],\n            f\"{book['avg_rating']:.1f}\"\n        ])\n        lines.append(line)\n    with open(BOOKS_FILE, 'w', encoding='utf-8') as f:\n        f.write('\\n'.join(lines))\n\n\ndef read_borrowings():\n    borrowings = {}\n    if not os.path.exists(BORROWINGS_FILE):\n        return borrowings\n    with open(BORROWINGS_FILE, 'r', encoding='utf-8') as f:\n        for line in f:\n            line = line.strip()\n            if not line:\n                continue\n            parts = line.split('|')\n            if len(parts) != 8:\n                continue\n            try:\n                borrow_id = int(parts[0])\n                username = parts[1]\n                book_id = int(parts[2])\n                borrow_date = parts[3]\n                due_date = parts[4]\n                return_date = parts[5] if parts[5] else None\n                status = parts[6]\n                fine_amount = float(parts[7])\n                borrowings[borrow_id] = {\n                    'borrow_id': borrow_id,\n                    'username': username,\n                    'book_id': book_id,\n                    'borrow_date': borrow_date,\n                    'due_date': due_date,\n                    'return_date': return_date,\n                    'status': status,\n                    'fine_amount': fine_amount\n                }\n            except ValueError:\n                continue\n    return borrowings\n\n\ndef write_borrowings(borrowings):\n    lines = []\n    for borrow in borrowings.values():\n        return_date_str = borrow['return_date'] if borrow['return_date'] else ''\n        line = '|'.join([\n            str(borrow['borrow_id']),\n            borrow['username'],\n            str(borrow['book_id']),\n            borrow['borrow_date'],\n            borrow['due_date'],\n            return_date_str,\n            borrow['status'],\n            f\"{borrow['fine_amount']:.2f}\"\n        ])\n        lines.append(line)\n    with open(BORROWINGS_FILE, 'w', encoding='utf-8') as f:\n        f.write('\\n'.join(lines))\n\n\ndef read_reservations():\n    reservations = {}\n    if not os.path.exists(RESERVATIONS_FILE):\n        return reservations\n    with open(RESERVATIONS_FILE, 'r', encoding='utf-8') as f:\n        for line in f:\n            line = line.strip()\n            if not line:\n                continue\n            parts = line.split('|')\n            if len(parts) != 5:\n                continue\n            try:\n                reservation_id = int(parts[0])\n                username = parts[1]\n                book_id = int(parts[2])\n                reservation_date = parts[3]\n                status = parts[4]\n                reservations[reservation_id] = {\n                    'reservation_id': reservation_id,\n                    'username': username,\n                    'book_id': book_id,\n                    'reservation_date': reservation_date,\n                    'status': status\n                }\n            except ValueError:\n                continue\n    return reservations\n\n\ndef write_reservations(reservations):\n    lines = []\n    for res in reservations.values():\n        line = '|'.join([\n            str(res['reservation_id']),\n            res['username'],\n            str(res['book_id']),\n            res['reservation_date'],\n            res['status']\n        ])\n        lines.append(line)\n    with open(RESERVATIONS_FILE, 'w', encoding='utf-8') as f:\n        f.write('\\n'.join(lines))\n\n\ndef read_reviews():\n    reviews = {}\n    if not os.path.exists(REVIEWS_FILE):\n        return reviews\n    with open(REVIEWS_FILE, 'r', encoding='utf-8') as f:\n        for line in f:\n            line = line.strip()\n            if not line:\n                continue\n            parts = line.split('|')\n            if len(parts) != 6:\n                continue\n            try:\n                review_id = int(parts[0])\n                username = parts[1]\n                book_id = int(parts[2])\n                rating = int(parts[3])\n                review_text = parts[4]\n                review_date = parts[5]\n                reviews[review_id] = {\n                    'review_id': review_id,\n                    'username': username,\n                    'book_id': book_id,\n                    'rating': rating,\n                    'review_text': review_text,\n                    'review_date': review_date\n                }\n            except ValueError:\n                continue\n    return reviews\n\n\ndef write_reviews(reviews):\n    lines = []\n    for rev in reviews.values():\n        line = '|'.join([\n            str(rev['review_id']),\n            rev['username'],\n            str(rev['book_id']),\n            str(rev['rating']),\n            rev['review_text'],\n            rev['review_date']\n        ])\n        lines.append(line)\n    with open(REVIEWS_FILE, 'w', encoding='utf-8') as f:\n        f.write('\\n'.join(lines))\n\n\ndef read_fines():\n    fines = {}\n    if not os.path.exists(FINES_FILE):\n        return fines\n    with open(FINES_FILE, 'r', encoding='utf-8') as f:\n        for line in f:\n            line = line.strip()\n            if not line:\n                continue\n            parts = line.split('|')\n            if len(parts) != 6:\n                continue\n            try:\n                fine_id = int(parts[0])\n                username = parts[1]\n                borrow_id = int(parts[2])\n                amount = float(parts[3])\n                status = parts[4]\n                date_issued = parts[5]\n                fines[fine_id] = {\n                    'fine_id': fine_id,\n                    'username': username,\n                    'borrow_id': borrow_id,\n                    'amount': amount,\n                    'status': status,\n                    'date_issued': date_issued\n                }\n            except ValueError:\n                continue\n    return fines\n\n\ndef write_fines(fines):\n    lines = []\n    for fine in fines.values():\n        line = '|'.join([\n            str(fine['fine_id']),\n            fine['username'],\n            str(fine['borrow_id']),\n            f\"{fine['amount']:.2f}\",\n            fine['status'],\n            fine['date_issued']\n        ])\n        lines.append(line)\n    with open(FINES_FILE, 'w', encoding='utf-8') as f:\n        f.write('\\n'.join(lines))\n\n\n# ------------------------------------------------------------------\n# For simplicity, simulate a logged-in user\n# In real application, use proper authentication and sessions\nLOGGED_IN_USERNAME = 'john_reader'\n\n# Utility for date handling\n\ndef get_current_date():\n    return datetime.utcnow().date()\n\ndef date_to_str(date_obj):\n    return date_obj.strftime('%Y-%m-%d')\n\ndef str_to_date(date_str):\n    try:\n        return datetime.strptime(date_str, '%Y-%m-%d').date()\n    except:\n        return None\n\n# Calculate fine based on days overdue\n# Let's say fine is $1 per day overdue\n\ndef calculate_fine(due_date_str, return_date_str=None):\n    due_date = str_to_date(due_date_str)\n    if due_date is None:\n        return 0.0\n    if return_date_str:\n        return_date = str_to_date(return_date_str)\n        if return_date is None:\n            return 0.0\n    else:\n        return_date = get_current_date()\n\n    delta = (return_date - due_date).days\n    if delta > 0:\n        return float(delta)  # $1 per day\n    return 0.0\n\n# Update borrowings status and fines\n\ndef refresh_borrowings_status():\n    borrowings = read_borrowings()\n    fines = read_fines()\n    fine_updated = False\n    for borrow in borrowings.values():\n        if borrow['status'] == 'Active':\n            due_date_obj = str_to_date(borrow['due_date'])\n            today = get_current_date()\n            if due_date_obj and today > due_date_obj:\n                borrow['status'] = 'Overdue'\n                # Calculate or update fine\n                fine_amount = calculate_fine(borrow['due_date'])\n                borrow['fine_amount'] = fine_amount\n\n                # Add fine record if not exist or update\n                fine_found = False\n                for fine in fines.values():\n                    if fine['borrow_id'] == borrow['borrow_id'] and fine['username'] == borrow['username'] and fine['status'] == 'Unpaid':\n                        if abs(fine['amount'] - fine_amount) > 0.01:\n                            fine['amount'] = fine_amount\n                            fine_updated = True\n                        fine_found = True\n                        break\n                if not fine_found and fine_amount > 0:\n                    new_fine_id = max(fines.keys()) + 1 if fines else 1\n                    fine = {\n                        'fine_id': new_fine_id,\n                        'username': borrow['username'],\n                        'borrow_id': borrow['borrow_id'],\n                        'amount': fine_amount,\n                        'status': 'Unpaid',\n                        'date_issued': date_to_str(today)\n                    }\n                    fines[new_fine_id] = fine\n                    fine_updated = True\n            else:\n                # If before due date, no fine\n                borrow['fine_amount'] = 0.0\n\n    if fine_updated:\n        write_fines(fines)\n    write_borrowings(borrowings)\n\n\n# Route 1: Root redirect\n@app.route('/')\ndef root_redirect():\n    return redirect(url_for('dashboard'))\n\n# Route 2: Dashboard\n@app.route('/dashboard')\ndef dashboard():\n    username = LOGGED_IN_USERNAME\n    # Confirm user exists\n    users = read_users()\n    if username not in users:\n        flash('User not found.', 'error')\n        return redirect(url_for('root_redirect'))\n    return render_template('dashboard.html', username=username)\n\n# Route 3: Book Catalog\n@app.route('/catalog')\ndef book_catalog():\n    books = list(read_books().values())\n    # We can pass all, frontend can handle display\n    # Just send book_id, title, author, status\n    books_for_template = [{\n        'book_id': b['book_id'],\n        'title': b['title'],\n        'author': b['author'],\n        'status': b['status']\n    } for b in books]\n    return render_template('catalog.html', books=books_for_template)\n\n# Route 4: Book Details\n@app.route('/book/<int:book_id>')\ndef book_details(book_id):\n    books = read_books()\n    if book_id not in books:\n        flash('Book not found.', 'error')\n        return redirect(url_for('book_catalog'))\n    book = books[book_id]\n\n    reviews = read_reviews().values()\n    book_reviews = []\n    for r in reviews:\n        if r['book_id'] == book_id:\n            book_reviews.append({\n                'review_id': r['review_id'],\n                'username': r['username'],\n                'rating': r['rating'],\n                'review_text': r['review_text'],\n                'review_date': r['review_date']\n            })\n    # Sort reviews by review_date descending\n    book_reviews.sort(key=lambda x: x['review_date'], reverse=True)\n\n    return render_template('book_details.html', book=book, reviews=book_reviews)\n\n# Route 5 (GET): Borrow Confirmation GET\n@app.route('/borrow/<int:book_id>', methods=['GET'])\ndef borrow_confirmation_get(book_id):\n    books = read_books()\n    if book_id not in books:\n        flash('Book not found.', 'error')\n        return redirect(url_for('book_catalog'))\n    book = books[book_id]\n    due_date_obj = get_current_date() + timedelta(days=14)\n    due_date_str = date_to_str(due_date_obj)\n    book_brief = {\n        'book_id': book['book_id'],\n        'title': book['title']\n    }\n    return render_template('borrow_confirmation.html', book=book_brief, due_date=due_date_str)\n\n# Route 6 (POST): Borrow Confirmation POST\n@app.route('/borrow/<int:book_id>', methods=['POST'])\ndef borrow_confirmation_post(book_id):\n    books = read_books()\n    users = read_users()\n    borrowings = read_borrowings()\n\n    if LOGGED_IN_USERNAME not in users:\n        flash('User not found.', 'error')\n        return redirect(url_for('dashboard'))\n\n    if book_id not in books:\n        flash('Book not found.', 'error')\n        return redirect(url_for('book_catalog'))\n\n    book = books[book_id]\n\n    # Check book availability\n    if book['status'] != 'Available':\n        flash('Book is not available for borrowing.', 'error')\n        due_date_obj = get_current_date() + timedelta(days=14)\n        due_date_str = date_to_str(due_date_obj)\n        book_brief = {'book_id': book['book_id'], 'title': book['title']}\n        return render_template('borrow_confirmation.html', book=book_brief, due_date=due_date_str)\n\n    # Check if user already has active or overdue borrow for this book\n    for b in borrowings.values():\n        if (b['username'] == LOGGED_IN_USERNAME and b['book_id'] == book_id and \n            b['status'] in ('Active', 'Overdue')):\n            flash('You have already borrowed this book and it is not yet returned.', 'error')\n            due_date_obj = get_current_date() + timedelta(days=14)\n            due_date_str = date_to_str(due_date_obj)\n            book_brief = {'book_id': book['book_id'], 'title': book['title']}\n            return render_template('borrow_confirmation.html', book=book_brief, due_date=due_date_str)\n\n    # Proceed with borrowing\n    new_borrow_id = max(borrowings.keys()) + 1 if borrowings else 1\n    borrow_date_str = date_to_str(get_current_date())\n    due_date_obj = get_current_date() + timedelta(days=14)\n    due_date_str = date_to_str(due_date_obj)\n\n    new_borrow = {\n        'borrow_id': new_borrow_id,\n        'username': LOGGED_IN_USERNAME,\n        'book_id': book_id,\n        'borrow_date': borrow_date_str,\n        'due_date': due_date_str,\n        'return_date': None,\n        'status': 'Active',\n        'fine_amount': 0.0\n    }\n\n    borrowings[new_borrow_id] = new_borrow\n\n    # Update book status to Borrowed\n    book['status'] = 'Borrowed'\n    books[book_id] = book\n\n    # Write updated data\n    write_borrowings(borrowings)\n    write_books(books)\n\n    flash('Book borrowed successfully.', 'success')\n    return redirect(url_for('my_borrowings'))\n\n# Route 7: My Borrowings\n@app.route('/my-borrows')\ndef my_borrowings():\n    refresh_borrowings_status()  # Update statuses and fines\n    borrowings = read_borrowings()\n    books = read_books()\n\n    # We only show borrowings for current user\n    user_borrowings = [b for b in borrowings.values() if b['username'] == LOGGED_IN_USERNAME]\n\n    filter_status = request.args.get('filter_status', '')\n    if filter_status:\n        filter_status = filter_status.capitalize()\n        user_borrowings = [b for b in user_borrowings if b['status'] == filter_status]\n\n    borrow_list = []\n    for b in user_borrowings:\n        book = books.get(b['book_id'])\n        title = book['title'] if book else 'Unknown'\n        borrow_list.append({\n            'borrow_id': b['borrow_id'],\n            'title': title,\n            'borrow_date': b['borrow_date'],\n            'due_date': b['due_date'],\n            'status': b['status']\n        })\n\n    return render_template('my_borrows.html', borrowings=borrow_list, filter_status=filter_status)\n\n# Route 8: Return book\n@app.route('/return/<int:borrow_id>', methods=['POST'])\ndef return_book(borrow_id):\n    borrowings = read_borrowings()\n    books = read_books()\n\n    if borrow_id not in borrowings:\n        flash('Borrowing record not found.', 'error')\n        return redirect(url_for('my_borrowings'))\n\n    borrow = borrowings[borrow_id]\n    if borrow['username'] != LOGGED_IN_USERNAME:\n        flash('You cannot return a borrow that is not yours.', 'error')\n        return redirect(url_for('my_borrowings'))\n\n    if borrow['status'] == 'Returned':\n        flash('This book has already been returned.', 'info')\n        return redirect(url_for('my_borrowings'))\n\n    # Update borrow return date and status\n    return_date_str = date_to_str(get_current_date())\n    borrow['return_date'] = return_date_str\n\n    # Update status depending on if overdue or not\n    due_date_obj = str_to_date(borrow['due_date'])\n    return_date_obj = str_to_date(return_date_str)\n\n    if return_date_obj and due_date_obj and return_date_obj > due_date_obj:\n        borrow['status'] = 'Returned'  # Returned but was overdue\n    else:\n        borrow['status'] = 'Returned'\n\n    # Update book status to Available\n    book_id = borrow['book_id']\n    book = books.get(book_id)\n    if book:\n        # Check if there are active reservations for this book\n        reservations = read_reservations()\n        has_active_reservation = any(\n            r['book_id'] == book_id and r['status'] == 'Active' for r in reservations.values())\n        if has_active_reservation:\n            book['status'] = 'Reserved'\n        else:\n            book['status'] = 'Available'\n\n        books[book_id] = book\n\n    # Clear fine_amount for borrowing if returned\n    borrow['fine_amount'] = 0.0\n\n    # Write updates\n    borrowings[borrow_id] = borrow\n    write_borrowings(borrowings)\n    write_books(books)\n\n    flash('Book returned successfully.', 'success')\n    return redirect(url_for('my_borrowings'))\n\n# Route 9: My Reservations\n@app.route('/my-reservations')\ndef my_reservations():\n    reservations = read_reservations()\n    books = read_books()\n\n    user_reservations = [r for r in reservations.values() if r['username'] == LOGGED_IN_USERNAME]\n    reservations_list = []\n    for r in user_reservations:\n        book = books.get(r['book_id'])\n        title = book['title'] if book else 'Unknown'\n        reservations_list.append({\n            'reservation_id': r['reservation_id'],\n            'title': title,\n            'reservation_date': r['reservation_date'],\n            'status': r['status']\n        })\n\n    return render_template('my_reservations.html', reservations=reservations_list)\n\n# Route 10: Cancel Reservation\n@app.route('/cancel-reservation/<int:reservation_id>', methods=['POST'])\ndef cancel_reservation(reservation_id):\n    reservations = read_reservations()\n\n    if reservation_id not in reservations:\n        flash('Reservation not found.', 'error')\n        return redirect(url_for('my_reservations'))\n\n    reservation = reservations[reservation_id]\n\n    if reservation['username'] != LOGGED_IN_USERNAME:\n        flash('You can only cancel your own reservations.', 'error')\n        return redirect(url_for('my_reservations'))\n\n    if reservation['status'] == 'Cancelled':\n        flash('Reservation already cancelled.', 'info')\n        return redirect(url_for('my_reservations'))\n\n    # Cancel the reservation\n    reservation['status'] = 'Cancelled'\n    reservations[reservation_id] = reservation\n    write_reservations(reservations)\n\n    # Update book status if needed\n    books = read_books()\n    book = books.get(reservation['book_id'])\n    if book:\n        # Check if there are any other active reservations\n        has_other_active = any(\n            r for r in reservations.values()\n            if r['book_id'] == book['book_id'] and r['status'] == 'Active'\n        )\n        if not has_other_active and book['status'] == 'Reserved':\n            book['status'] = 'Available'\n            books[book['book_id']] = book\n            write_books(books)\n\n    flash('Reservation cancelled successfully.', 'success')\n    return redirect(url_for('my_reservations'))\n\n# Route 11: My Reviews\n@app.route('/my-reviews')\ndef my_reviews():\n    reviews = read_reviews()\n    books = read_books()\n\n    user_reviews = [r for r in reviews.values() if r['username'] == LOGGED_IN_USERNAME]\n    review_list = []\n    for r in user_reviews:\n        book = books.get(r['book_id'])\n        book_title = book['title'] if book else 'Unknown'\n        review_list.append({\n            'review_id': r['review_id'],\n            'book_title': book_title,\n            'rating': r['rating'],\n            'review_text': r['review_text']\n        })\n\n    return render_template('my_reviews.html', reviews=review_list)\n\n# Route 12: Write/Edit Review (GET)\n@app.route('/write-review/<int:book_id>', methods=['GET'])\n@app.route('/edit-review/<int:review_id>', methods=['GET'])\ndef write_edit_review_get(book_id=None, review_id=None):\n    books = read_books()\n    reviews = read_reviews()\n\n    if book_id is not None:\n        # Writing new review for book_id\n        if book_id not in books:\n            flash('Book not found.', 'error')\n            return redirect(url_for('book_catalog'))\n        book = books[book_id]\n        # Check if user already has a review for this book\n        existing_review = None\n        for r in reviews.values():\n            if r['book_id'] == book_id and r['username'] == LOGGED_IN_USERNAME:\n                existing_review = r\n                break\n        # If existing review found, redirect to edit review\n        if existing_review:\n            return redirect(url_for('write_edit_review_get', review_id=existing_review['review_id']))\n\n        book_brief = {'book_id': book['book_id'], 'title': book['title']}\n        return render_template('write_review.html', book=book_brief, existing_review=None)\n\n    if review_id is not None:\n        # Editing existing review\n        if review_id not in reviews:\n            flash('Review not found.', 'error')\n            return redirect(url_for('my_reviews'))\n        review = reviews[review_id]\n        if review['username'] != LOGGED_IN_USERNAME:\n            flash('You can only edit your own reviews.', 'error')\n            return redirect(url_for('my_reviews'))\n        book = books.get(review['book_id'])\n        if not book:\n            flash('Book not found for this review.', 'error')\n            return redirect(url_for('my_reviews'))\n        book_brief = {'book_id': book['book_id'], 'title': book['title']}\n        existing_review = {\n            'review_id': review['review_id'],\n            'rating': review['rating'],\n            'review_text': review['review_text']\n        }\n        return render_template('write_review.html', book=book_brief, existing_review=existing_review)\n\n    # If neither, redirect to dashboard\n    return redirect(url_for('dashboard'))\n\n# Route 13: Write/Edit Review (POST)\n@app.route('/write-review/<int:book_id>', methods=['POST'])\n@app.route('/edit-review/<int:review_id>', methods=['POST'])\ndef write_edit_review_post(book_id=None, review_id=None):\n    rating = request.form.get('rating')\n    review_text = request.form.get('review_text')\n\n    # Validation\n    error = None\n    try:\n        rating_int = int(rating)\n        if rating_int < 1 or rating_int > 5:\n            error = 'Rating must be between 1 and 5.'\n    except:\n        error = 'Invalid rating value.'\n\n    if not review_text or review_text.strip() == '':\n        error = 'Review text cannot be empty.'\n\n    books = read_books()\n    reviews = read_reviews()\n\n    if book_id is not None:\n        # Creating new review\n        if book_id not in books:\n            flash('Book not found.', 'error')\n            return redirect(url_for('book_catalog'))\n\n        # Check if user already has review\n        existing = None\n        for r in reviews.values():\n            if r['book_id'] == book_id and r['username'] == LOGGED_IN_USERNAME:\n                existing = r\n                break\n        if existing:\n            flash('Review already exists. Please edit it instead.', 'error')\n            return redirect(url_for('write_edit_review_get', review_id=existing['review_id']))\n\n        if error:\n            book_brief = {'book_id': book_id, 'title': books[book_id]['title']}\n            return render_template('write_review.html', book=book_brief, existing_review=None, error=error)\n\n        # Add new review\n        new_review_id = max(reviews.keys()) + 1 if reviews else 1\n        today_str = date_to_str(get_current_date())\n\n        new_review = {\n            'review_id': new_review_id,\n            'username': LOGGED_IN_USERNAME,\n            'book_id': book_id,\n            'rating': rating_int,\n            'review_text': review_text.strip(),\n            'review_date': today_str\n        }\n        reviews[new_review_id] = new_review\n\n        # Update average rating of book\n        update_book_avg_rating(book_id, reviews, books)\n\n        write_reviews(reviews)\n        write_books(books)\n\n        flash('Review added successfully.', 'success')\n        return redirect(url_for('book_details', book_id=book_id))\n\n    if review_id is not None:\n        # Editing existing review\n        if review_id not in reviews:\n            flash('Review not found.', 'error')\n            return redirect(url_for('my_reviews'))\n        review = reviews[review_id]\n\n        if review['username'] != LOGGED_IN_USERNAME:\n            flash('You can only edit your own reviews.', 'error')\n            return redirect(url_for('my_reviews'))\n\n        if error:\n            book = books.get(review['book_id'])\n            if not book:\n                flash('Book not found.', 'error')\n                return redirect(url_for('my_reviews'))\n            book_brief = {'book_id': book['book_id'], 'title': book['title']}\n            existing_review = {'rating': rating_int if not error else None, 'review_text': review_text}\n            return render_template('write_review.html', book=book_brief, existing_review=existing_review, error=error)\n\n        # Update data\n        review['rating'] = rating_int\n        review['review_text'] = review_text.strip()\n        review['review_date'] = date_to_str(get_current_date())\n\n        # Update average rating\n        update_book_avg_rating(review['book_id'], reviews, books)\n\n        reviews[review_id] = review\n        write_reviews(reviews)\n        write_books(books)\n\n        flash('Review updated successfully.', 'success')\n        return redirect(url_for('book_details', book_id=review['book_id']))\n\n    return redirect(url_for('dashboard'))\n\n# Utility to update book average rating\n\ndef update_book_avg_rating(book_id, reviews, books):\n    ratings = [r['rating'] for r in reviews.values() if r['book_id'] == book_id]\n    if ratings:\n        avg = sum(ratings) / len(ratings)\n    else:\n        avg = 0.0\n    book = books.get(book_id)\n    if book:\n        book['avg_rating'] = round(avg, 1)\n\n# Route 14: Delete Review\n@app.route('/delete-review/<int:review_id>', methods=['POST'])\ndef delete_review(review_id):\n    reviews = read_reviews()\n\n    if review_id not in reviews:\n        flash('Review not found.', 'error')\n        return redirect(url_for('my_reviews'))\n\n    review = reviews[review_id]\n    if review['username'] != LOGGED_IN_USERNAME:\n        flash('You can only delete your own reviews.', 'error')\n        return redirect(url_for('my_reviews'))\n\n    book_id = review['book_id']\n    del reviews[review_id]\n\n    # After delete, update average rating\n    books = read_books()\n    update_book_avg_rating(book_id, reviews, books)\n\n    write_reviews(reviews)\n    write_books(books)\n\n    flash('Review deleted successfully.', 'success')\n    return redirect(url_for('my_reviews'))\n\n# Route 15: User Profile (GET)\n@app.route('/profile', methods=['GET'])\ndef user_profile():\n    users = read_users()\n    borrowings = read_borrowings()\n    books = read_books()\n\n    if LOGGED_IN_USERNAME not in users:\n        flash('User not found.', 'error')\n        return redirect(url_for('dashboard'))\n\n    user = users[LOGGED_IN_USERNAME]\n    # Borrow history = all borrowings that user had\n    borrow_history = []\n    for b in borrowings.values():\n        if b['username'] == LOGGED_IN_USERNAME:\n            book = books.get(b['book_id'])\n            title = book['title'] if book else 'Unknown'\n            borrow_history.append({\n                'title': title,\n                'borrow_date': b['borrow_date'],\n                'return_date': b['return_date']\n            })\n\n    return render_template('profile.html', username=LOGGED_IN_USERNAME, email=user['email'], borrow_history=borrow_history)\n\n# Route 16: Update Profile (POST)\n@app.route('/profile', methods=['POST'])\ndef update_profile():\n    users = read_users()\n\n    if LOGGED_IN_USERNAME not in users:\n        flash('User not found.', 'error')\n        return redirect(url_for('dashboard'))\n\n    user = users[LOGGED_IN_USERNAME]\n\n    new_email = request.form.get('email', '').strip()\n    new_phone = request.form.get('phone', '').strip()\n    new_address = request.form.get('address', '').strip()\n\n    # Validate email\n    if not new_email:\n        flash('Email cannot be empty.', 'error')\n        return redirect(url_for('user_profile'))\n\n    # Update\n    user['email'] = new_email\n    user['phone'] = new_phone\n    user['address'] = new_address\n    users[LOGGED_IN_USERNAME] = user\n\n    write_users(users)\n    flash('Profile updated successfully.', 'success')\n\n    return redirect(url_for('user_profile'))\n\n# Route 17: Payment Confirmation (GET)\n@app.route('/payment/<int:fine_id>', methods=['GET'])\ndef payment_confirmation_get(fine_id):\n    fines = read_fines()\n\n    if fine_id not in fines:\n        flash('Fine record not found.', 'error')\n        return redirect(url_for('user_profile'))\n\n    fine = fines[fine_id]\n    if fine['username'] != LOGGED_IN_USERNAME:\n        flash('You can only pay your own fines.', 'error')\n        return redirect(url_for('user_profile'))\n\n    fine_amount = fine['amount']\n    return render_template('payment_confirmation.html', fine_amount=fine_amount, fine_id=fine_id)\n\n# Route 18: Confirm Payment (POST)\n@app.route('/payment/<int:fine_id>', methods=['POST'])\ndef payment_confirmation_post(fine_id):\n    fines = read_fines()\n\n    if fine_id not in fines:\n        flash('Fine record not found.', 'error')\n        return redirect(url_for('user_profile'))\n\n    fine = fines[fine_id]\n    if fine['username'] != LOGGED_IN_USERNAME:\n        flash('You can only pay your own fines.', 'error')\n        return redirect(url_for('user_profile'))\n\n    if fine['status'] == 'Paid':\n        flash('This fine is already paid.', 'info')\n        return redirect(url_for('user_profile'))\n\n    # Mark fine as paid\n    fine['status'] = 'Paid'\n    fines[fine_id] = fine\n    write_fines(fines)\n\n    flash('Payment successful. Thank you!', 'success')\n    return redirect(url_for('user_profile'))\n\n\nif __name__ == '__main__':\n    app.run(debug=True)\n"})

  [app.py] from flask import Flask, render_template, redirect, url_for, request, flash import os from datetime import datetime, timedelta  app = Flask(__name__) app.secret_key = 'your_secret_key_here'  # Replace with a proper secret key in production  # Data file paths DATA_DIR = 'data' USERS_FILE = os.path.join(DATA_DIR, 'users.txt') BOOKS_FILE = os.path.join(DATA_DIR, 'books.txt') BORROWINGS_FILE = os.path.join(DATA_DIR, 'borrowings.txt') RESERVATIONS_FILE = os.path.join(DATA_DIR, 'reservations.txt') REVIEWS_FILE = os.path.join(DATA_DIR, 'reviews.txt') FINES_FILE = os.path.join(DATA_DIR, 'fines.txt')  # Helper functions for reading and writing pipe-delimited files  def read_users():     users = {}     if not os.path.exists(USERS_FILE):         return users     with open(USERS_FILE, 'r', encoding='utf-8') as f:         for line in f:             line = line.strip()             if not line:                 continue             parts = line.split('|')             if len(parts) != 4:                 continue             username, email, phone, address = parts             users[username] = {                 'username': username,                 'email': email,                 'phone': phone,                 'address': address             }     return users  def write_users(users):     lines = []     for user in users.values():         line = '|'.join([user['username'], user['email'], user['phone'], user['address']])         lines.append(line)     with open(USERS_FILE, 'w', encoding='utf-8') as f:         f.write('\n'.join(lines))   def read_books():     books = {}     if not os.path.exists(BOOKS_FILE):         return books     with open(BOOKS_FILE, 'r', encoding='utf-8') as f:         for line in f:             line = line.strip()             if not line:                 continue             parts = line.split('|')             if len(parts) != 10:                 continue             try:                 book_id = int(parts[0])                 title = parts[1]                 author = parts[2]                 isbn = parts[3]                 genre = parts[4]                 publisher = parts[5]                 year = int(parts[6])                 description = parts[7]                 status = parts[8]                 avg_rating = float(parts[9])                 books[book_id] = {                     'book_id': book_id,                     'title': title,                     'author': author,                     'isbn': isbn,                     'genre': genre,                     'publisher': publisher,                     'year': year,                     'description': description,                     'status': status,                     'avg_rating': avg_rating                 }             except ValueError:                 continue     return books   def write_books(books):     lines = []     for book in books.values():         line = '|'.join([             str(book['book_id']),             book['title'],             book['author'],             book['isbn'],             book['genre'],             book['publisher'],             str(book['year']),             book['description'],             book['status'],             f"{book['avg_rating']:.1f}"         ])         lines.append(line)     with open(BOOKS_FILE, 'w', encoding='utf-8') as f:         f.write('\n'.join(lines))   def read_borrowings():     borrowings = {}     if not os.path.exists(BORROWINGS_FILE):         return borrowings     with open(BORROWINGS_FILE, 'r', encoding='utf-8') as f:         for line in f:             line = line.strip()             if not line:                 continue             parts = line.split('|')             if len(parts) != 8:                 continue             try:                 borrow_id = int(parts[0])                 username = parts[1]                 book_id = int(parts[2])                 borrow_date = parts[3]                 due_date = parts[4]                 return_date = parts[5] if parts[5] else None                 status = parts[6]                 fine_amount = float(parts[7])                 borrowings[borrow_id] = {                     'borrow_id': borrow_id,                     'username': username,                     'book_id': book_id,                     'borrow_date': borrow_date,                     'due_date': due_date,                     'return_date': return_date,                     'status': status,                     'fine_amount': fine_amount                 }             except ValueError:                 continue     return borrowings   def write_borrowings(borrowings):     lines = []     for borrow in borrowings.values():         return_date_str = borrow['return_date'] if borrow['return_date'] else ''         line = '|'.join([             str(borrow['borrow_id']),             borrow['username'],             str(borrow['book_id']),             borrow['borrow_date'],             borrow['due_date'],             return_date_str,             borrow['status'],             f"{borrow['fine_amount']:.2f}"         ])         lines.append(line)     with open(BORROWINGS_FILE, 'w', encoding='utf-8') as f:         f.write('\n'.join(lines))   def read_reservations():     reservations = {}     if not os.path.exists(RESERVATIONS_FILE):         return reservations     with open(RESERVATIONS_FILE, 'r', encoding='utf-8') as f:         for line in f:             line = line.strip()             if not line:                 continue             parts = line.split('|')             if len(parts) != 5:                 continue             try:                 reservation_id = int(parts[0])                 username = parts[1]                 book_id = int(parts[2])                 reservation_date = parts[3]                 status = parts[4]                 reservations[reservation_id] = {                     'reservation_id': reservation_id,                     'username': username,                     'book_id': book_id,                     'reservation_date': reservation_date,                     'status': status                 }             except ValueError:                 continue     return reservations   def write_reservations(reservations):     lines = []     for res in reservations.values():         line = '|'.join([             str(res['reservation_id']),             res['username'],             str(res['book_id']),             res['reservation_date'],             res['status']         ])         lines.append(line)     with open(RESERVATIONS_FILE, 'w', encoding='utf-8') as f:         f.write('\n'.join(lines))   def read_reviews():     reviews = {}     if not os.path.exists(REVIEWS_FILE):         return reviews     with open(REVIEWS_FILE, 'r', encoding='utf-8') as f:         for line in f:             line = line.strip()             if not line:                 continue             parts = line.split('|')             if len(parts) != 6:                 continue             try:                 review_id = int(parts[0])                 username = parts[1]                 book_id = int(parts[2])                 rating = int(parts[3])                 review_text = parts[4]                 review_date = parts[5]                 reviews[review_id] = {                     'review_id': review_id,                     'username': username,                     'book_id': book_id,                     'rating': rating,                     'review_text': review_text,                     'review_date': review_date                 }             except ValueError:                 continue     return reviews   def write_reviews(reviews):     lines = []     for rev in reviews.values():         line = '|'.join([             str(rev['review_id']),             rev['username'],             str(rev['book_id']),             str(rev['rating']),             rev['review_text'],             rev['review_date']         ])         lines.append(line)     with open(REVIEWS_FILE, 'w', encoding='utf-8') as f:         f.write('\n'.join(lines))   def read_fines():     fines = {}     if not os.path.exists(FINES_FILE):         return fines     with open(FINES_FILE, 'r', encoding='utf-8') as f:         for line in f:             line = line.strip()             if not line:                 continue             parts = line.split('|')             if len(parts) != 6:                 continue             try:                 fine_id = int(parts[0])                 username = parts[1]                 borrow_id = int(parts[2])                 amount = float(parts[3])                 status = parts[4]                 date_issued = parts[5]                 fines[fine_id] = {                     'fine_id': fine_id,                     'username': username,                     'borrow_id': borrow_id,                     'amount': amount,                     'status': status,                     'date_issued': date_issued                 }             except ValueError:                 continue     return fines   def write_fines(fines):     lines = []     for fine in fines.values():         line = '|'.join([             str(fine['fine_id']),             fine['username'],             str(fine['borrow_id']),             f"{fine['amount']:.2f}",             fine['status'],             fine['date_issued']         ])         lines.append(line)     with open(FINES_FILE, 'w', encoding='utf-8') as f:         f.write('\n'.join(lines))   # ------------------------------------------------------------------ # For simplicity, simulate a logged-in user # In real application, use proper authentication and sessions LOGGED_IN_USERNAME = 'john_reader'  # Utility for date handling  def get_current_date():     return datetime.utcnow().date()  def date_to_str(date_obj):     return date_obj.strftime('%Y-%m-%d')  def str_to_date(date_str):     try:         return datetime.strptime(date_str, '%Y-%m-%d').date()     except:         return None  # Calculate fine based on days overdue # Let's say fine is $1 per day overdue  def calculate_fine(due_date_str, return_date_str=None):     due_date = str_to_date(due_date_str)     if due_date is None:         return 0.0     if return_date_str:         return_date = str_to_date(return_date_str)         if return_date is None:             return 0.0     else:         return_date = get_current_date()      delta = (return_date - due_date).days     if delta > 0:         return float(delta)  # $1 per day     return 0.0  # Update borrowings status and fines  def refresh_borrowings_status():     borrowings = read_borrowings()     fines = read_fines()     fine_updated = False     for borrow in borrowings.values():         if borrow['status'] == 'Active':             due_date_obj = str_to_date(borrow['due_date'])             today = get_current_date()             if due_date_obj and today > due_date_obj:                 borrow['status'] = 'Overdue'                 # Calculate or update fine                 fine_amount = calculate_fine(borrow['due_date'])                 borrow['fine_amount'] = fine_amount                  # Add fine record if not exist or update                 fine_found = False                 for fine in fines.values():                     if fine['borrow_id'] == borrow['borrow_id'] and fine['username'] == borrow['username'] and fine['status'] == 'Unpaid':                         if abs(fine['amount'] - fine_amount) > 0.01:                             fine['amount'] = fine_amount                             fine_updated = True                         fine_found = True                         break                 if not fine_found and fine_amount > 0:                     new_fine_id = max(fines.keys()) + 1 if fines else 1                     fine = {                         'fine_id': new_fine_id,                         'username': borrow['username'],                         'borrow_id': borrow['borrow_id'],                         'amount': fine_amount,                         'status': 'Unpaid',                         'date_issued': date_to_str(today)                     }                     fines[new_fine_id] = fine                     fine_updated = True             else:                 # If before due date, no fine                 borrow['fine_amount'] = 0.0      if fine_updated:         write_fines(fines)     write_borrowings(borrowings)   # Route 1: Root redirect @app.route('/') def root_redirect():     return redirect(url_for('dashboard'))  # Route 2: Dashboard @app.route('/dashboard') def dashboard():     username = LOGGED_IN_USERNAME     # Confirm user exists     users = read_users()     if username not in users:         flash('User not found.', 'error')         return redirect(url_for('root_redirect'))     return render_template('dashboard.html', username=username)  # Route 3: Book Catalog @app.route('/catalog') def book_catalog():     books = list(read_books().values())     # We can pass all, frontend can handle display     # Just send book_id, title, author, status     books_for_template = [{         'book_id': b['book_id'],         'title': b['title'],         'author': b['author'],         'status': b['status']     } for b in books]     return render_template('catalog.html', books=books_for_template)  # Route 4: Book Details @app.route('/book/<int:book_id>') def book_details(book_id):     books = read_books()     if book_id not in books:         flash('Book not found.', 'error')         return book_reviews.append({                 'review_id': r['review_id'],                 'username': r['username'],                 'rating': r['rating'],                 'review_text': r['review_text'],                 'review_date': r['review_date']             })     # Sort reviews by review_date descending     book_reviews.sort(key=lambda x: x['review_date'], reverse=True)      return book['title']     }     return render_template('borrow_confirmation.html', book=book_brief, due_date=due_date_str)  # Route 6 (POST): Borrow Confirmation POST @app.route('/borrow/<int:book_id>', methods=['POST']) def borrow_confirmation_post(book_id):     books = read_books()     users = read_users()     borrowings = read_borrowings()      if LOGGED_IN_USERNAME not in users:         flash('User not found.', 'error')         return redirect(url_for('dashboard'))      if book_id not in books:         flash('Book not found.', 'error')         return redirect(url_for('book_catalog'))      book = books[book_id]      # Check book availability     if book['status'] != 'Available':         flash('Book is not available for borrowing.', 'error')         due_date_obj = get_current_date() + 1 if borrowings else 1     borrow_date_str = date_to_str(get_current_date())     due_date_obj = get_current_date() + timedelta(days=14)     due_date_str = date_to_str(due_date_obj)      new_borrow = books[book_id] = book      # Write updated data     write_borrowings(borrowings)     write_books(books)      flash('Book borrowed successfully.', 'success')     return read_borrowings()     books = read_books()      # We only show borrowings for current user     user_borrowings = [b for b in borrowings.values() if b['username'] == LOGGED_IN_USERNAME] filter_status]      borrow_list = []     for b in user_borrowings:         book = books.get(b['book_id'])         title = book['title'] if book else 'Unknown'         borrow_list.append({ render_template('my_borrows.html', borrowings=borrow_list, filter_status=filter_status)  # Route 8: Return book @app.route('/return/<int:borrow_id>', methods=['POST']) def return_book(borrow_id):     borrowings = read_borrowings()     books = read_books()      if borrow_id not in borrowings:         flash('Borrowing record not found.', 'error')         return redirect(url_for('my_borrowings'))      borrow = borrowings[borrow_id]     if borrow['username'] != LOGGED_IN_USERNAME:         flash('You cannot return a borrow that is not yours.', 'error')         return redirect(url_for('my_borrowings'))      if borrow['status'] == 'Returned':         flash('This book has already been returned.', 'info')         return redirect(url_for('my_borrowings'))      # Update borrow return date and status     return_date_str = date_to_str(get_current_date())     borrow['return_date'] = return_date_str      # Update status depending on if overdue or not     due_date_obj = str_to_date(borrow['due_date'])     return_date_obj = str_to_date(return_date_str)      if return_date_obj and due_date_obj and return_date_obj > due_date_obj:         borrow['status'] = 'Returned'  # Returned but was overdue     else:         borrow['status'] = 'Returned'      # Update book status to Available     book_id = borrow['book_id']     book = books.get(book_id)     if book:         # for r in reservations.values())         if has_active_reservation:             book['status'] = 'Reserved'         else:             book['status'] = 'Available'          books[book_id] = book      # Clear fine_amount for borrowing if returned     borrow['fine_amount'] = 0.0      # Write updates     borrowings[borrow_id] = borrow     write_borrowings(borrowings)     write_books(books)
