Route 11: My Reviews @app.route('/my-reviews') def my_reviews():     reviews = read_reviews()     books = read_books()      user_reviews = [r for r in reviews.values() if r['username'] == LOGGED_IN_USERNAME]     review_list = []     for r in user_reviews:         book = books.get(r['book_id'])         book_title = book['title'] if book else 'Unknown'         review_list.append({             'review_id': r['review_id'],             'book_title': book_title,             'rating': r['rating'],             'review_text': r['review_text']         })      return render_template('my_reviews.html', reviews=review_list)  # Route 12: Write/Edit Review (GET) @app.route('/write-review/<int:book_id>', methods=['GET']) @app.route('/edit-review/<int:review_id>', methods=['GET']) def write_edit_review_get(book_id=None, review_id=None):     books = read_books()     reviews = read_reviews()      if book_id is not None:         # Writing new review for book_id         if book_id not in books:             flash('Book not found.', 'error')             return redirect(url_for('book_catalog'))         book = books[book_id]         # Check if user already has a review for this book         existing_review = None         for r in reviews.values():             if r['book_id'] == book_id and r['username'] == LOGGED_IN_USERNAME:                 existing_review = r                 break         # If existing review found, redirect to edit review         if existing_review:             return redirect(url_for('write_edit_review_get', review_id=existing_review['review_id']))          book_brief = {'book_id': book['book_id'], 'title': book['title']}         return render_template('write_review.html', book=book_brief, existing_review=None)      if review_id is not None:         # Editing existing review         if review_id not in reviews:             flash('Review not found.', 'error')             return redirect(url_for('my_reviews'))         review = reviews[review_id]         if review['username'] != LOGGED_IN_USERNAME:             flash('You can only edit your own reviews.', 'error')             return redirect(url_for('my_reviews'))         book = books.get(review['book_id'])         if not book:             flash('Book not found for this review.', 'error')             return redirect(url_for('my_reviews'))         book_brief = {'book_id': book['book_id'], 'title': book['title']}         existing_review = {             'review_id': review['review_id'],             'rating': review['rating'],             'review_text': review['review_text']         }         return render_template('write_review.html', book=book_brief, existing_review=existing_review)      # If neither, redirect to dashboard     return redirect(url_for('dashboard'))  # Route 13: Write/Edit Review (POST) @app.route('/write-review/<int:book_id>', methods=['POST']) @app.route('/edit-review/<int:review_id>', methods=['POST']) def write_edit_review_post(book_id=None, review_id=None):     rating = request.form.get('rating')     review_text = request.form.get('review_text')      # Validation     error = None     try:         rating_int = int(rating)         if rating_int < 1 or rating_int > 5:             error = 'Rating must be between 1 and 5.'     except:         error = 'Invalid rating value.'      if not review_text or review_text.strip() == '':         error = 'Review text cannot be empty.'      books = read_books()     reviews = read_reviews()      if book_id is not None:         # Creating new review         if book_id not in books:             flash('Book not found.', 'error')             return redirect(url_for('book_catalog'))          # Check if user already has review         existing = None         for r in reviews.values():             if r['book_id'] == book_id and r['username'] == LOGGED_IN_USERNAME:                 existing = r                 break         if existing:             flash('Review already exists. Please edit it instead.', 'error')             return redirect(url_for('write_edit_review_get', review_id=existing['review_id']))          if error:             book_brief = {'book_id': book_id, 'title': books[book_id]['title']}             return render_template('write_review.html', book=book_brief, existing_review=None, error=error)          # Add new review         new_review_id = max(reviews.keys()) + 1 if reviews else 1         today_str = date_to_str(get_current_date())          new_review = {             'review_id': new_review_id,             'username': LOGGED_IN_USERNAME,             'book_id': book_id,             'rating': rating_int,             'review_text': review_text.strip(),             'review_date': today_str         }         reviews[new_review_id] = new_review          # Update average rating of book         update_book_avg_rating(book_id, reviews, books)          write_reviews(reviews)         write_books(books)          flash('Review added successfully.', 'success')         return redirect(url_for('book_details', book_id=book_id))      if review_id is not None:         # Editing existing review         if review_id not in reviews:             flash('Review not found.', 'error')             return redirect(url_for('my_reviews'))         review = reviews[review_id]          if review['username'] != LOGGED_IN_USERNAME:             flash('You can only edit your own reviews.', 'error')             return redirect(url_for('my_reviews'))          if error:             book = books.get(review['book_id'])             if not book:                 flash('Book not found.', 'error')                 return redirect(url_for('my_reviews'))             book_brief = {'book_id': book['book_id'], 'title': book['title']}             existing_review = {'rating': rating_int if not error else None, 'review_text': review_text}             return render_template('write_review.html', book=book_brief, existing_review=existing_review, error=error)          # Update data         review['rating'] = rating_int         review['review_text'] = review_text.strip()         review['review_date'] = date_to_str(get_current_date())          # Update average rating         update_book_avg_rating(review['book_id'], reviews, books)          reviews[review_id] = review         write_reviews(reviews)         write_books(books)          flash('Review updated successfully.', 'success')         return redirect(url_for('book_details', book_id=review['book_id']))      return redirect(url_for('dashboard'))  # Utility to update book average rating  def update_book_avg_rating(book_id, reviews, books):     ratings = [r['rating'] for r in reviews.values() if r['book_id'] == book_id]     if ratings:         avg = sum(ratings) / len(ratings)     else:         avg = 0.0     book = books.get(book_id)     if book:         book['avg_rating'] = round(avg, 1)  # Route 14: Delete Review @app.route('/delete-review/<int:review_id>', methods=['POST']) def delete_review(review_id):     reviews = read_reviews()      if review_id not in reviews:         flash('Review not found.', 'error')         return redirect(url_for('my_reviews'))      review = reviews[review_id]     if review['username'] != LOGGED_IN_USERNAME:         flash('You can only delete your own reviews.', 'error')         return redirect(url_for('my_reviews'))      book_id = review['book_id']     del reviews[review_id]      # After delete, update average rating     books = read_books()     update_book_avg_rating(book_id, reviews, books)      write_reviews(reviews)     write_books(books)      flash('Review deleted successfully.', 'success')     return redirect(url_for('my_reviews'))  # Route 15: User Profile (GET) @app.route('/profile', methods=['GET']) def user_profile():     users = read_users()     borrowings = read_borrowings()     books = read_books()      if LOGGED_IN_USERNAME not in users:         flash('User not found.', 'error')         return redirect(url_for('dashboard'))      user = users[LOGGED_IN_USERNAME]     # Borrow history = all borrowings that user had     borrow_history = []     for b in borrowings.values():         if b['username'] == LOGGED_IN_USERNAME:             book = books.get(b['book_id'])             title = book['title'] if book else 'Unknown'             borrow_history.append({                 'title': title,                 'borrow_date': b['borrow_date'],                 'return_date': b['return_date']             })      return render_template('profile.html', username=LOGGED_IN_USERNAME, email=user['email'], borrow_history=borrow_history)  # Route 16: Update Profile (POST) @app.route('/profile', methods=['POST']) def update_profile():     users = read_users()      if LOGGED_IN_USERNAME not in users:         flash('User not found.', 'error')         return redirect(url_for('dashboard'))      user = users[LOGGED_IN_USERNAME]      new_email = request.form.get('email', '').strip()     new_phone = request.form.get('phone', '').strip()     new_address = request.form.get('address', '').strip()      # Validate email     if not new_email:         flash('Email cannot be empty.', 'error')         return redirect(url_for('user_profile'))      # Update     user['email'] = new_email     user['phone'] = new_phone     user['address'] = new_address     users[LOGGED_IN_USERNAME] = user      write_users(users)     flash('Profile updated successfully.', 'success')      return redirect(url_for('user_profile'))  # Route 17: Payment Confirmation (GET) @app.route('/payment/<int:fine_id>', methods=['GET']) def payment_confirmation_get(fine_id):     fines = read_fines()      if fine_id not in fines:         flash('Fine record not found.', 'error')         return redirect(url_for('user_profile'))      fine = fines[fine_id]     if fine['username'] != LOGGED_IN_USERNAME:         flash('You can only pay your own fines.', 'error')         return redirect(url_for('user_profile'))      fine_amount = fine['amount']     return render_template('payment_confirmation.html', fine_amount=fine_amount, fine_id=fine_id)  # Route 18: Confirm Payment (POST) @app.route('/payment/<int:fine_id>', methods=['POST']) def payment_confirmation_post(fine_id):     fines = read_fines()      if fine_id not in fines:         flash('Fine record not found.', 'error')         return redirect(url_for('user_profile'))      fine = fines[fine_id]     if fine['username'] != LOGGED_IN_USERNAME:         flash('You can only pay your own fines.', 'error')         return redirect(url_for('user_profile'))      if fine['status'] == 'Paid':         flash('This fine is already paid.', 'info')         return redirect(url_for('user_profile'))      # Mark fine as paid     fine['status'] = 'Paid'     fines[fine_id] = fine     write_fines(fines)      flash('Payment successful. Thank you!', 'success')     return redirect(url_for('user_profile'))   if __name__ == '__main__':     app.run(debug=True)

  [test_report.md] # Test Report: OnlineLibrary Application  ## Summary Comprehensive functional, integration, and regression testing was conducted against the OnlineLibrary Flask application covering all main user flows, UI element verification, and data consistency checks as per design_spec.md. The tests were executed in a controlled environment with prepared sample data.  ## Test Cases and Results  | Test Case | Description | Expected Result | Actual Result | Status | |-----------|-------------|-----------------|---------------|--------| | Root Redirect | Access root URL `/` should redirect to `/dashboard` | Redirect response to `/dashboard` | Redirect response (`302 FOUND`) | Passed | | Dashboard Render | Access `/dashboard` renders dashboard page with username display | Page rendered with username present | Page rendered and username present | Passed | | Catalog Display | Access `/catalog` lists all available books | Catalog lists all sample books | All sample books listed | Passed | | Book Details Page | Access `/book/1` displays book details | Book details page includes book title | Book details page includes title | Passed | | Borrow Confirmation GET | Access `/borrow/1` GET shows borrow confirmation page | Page displays selected book info and due date | Page displays correct info | Passed | | Borrow Confirmation POST | POST `/borrow/1` borrows book if available | Book status updated, borrow record created, redirect to My Borrows | Book status is 'Borrowed', borrow created, redirect occurs | Passed | | My Borrowings Page | Access `/my-borrows` lists current user borrowings | Borrow list includes borrowed book | Borrow list includes borrowed book | Passed | | Return Book POST | POST return for active borrow marks as returned and updates book status | Borrow status updated to 'Returned', book status 'Available' | Borrow marked returned, book status available | Passed | | Write Review GET | Access write review page for book without existing review | Page displays review form for book | Review form displayed with correct book info | Passed | | Submit Review POST | Submit new review for a book | Review added, average rating updated | Review created and data updated | Passed | | Edit Review GET | Access edit review page for user's review | Page displays existing review data for editing | Correct review loaded in form | Passed | | Delete Review POST | Delete existing review | Review is removed from data | Review deleted successfully | Passed | | User Profile GET | Access user profile page | Profile page shows user info and borrow history | Profile page rendered with correct user info | Passed | | Update Profile POST | Update user profile with valid details | User data updated in users file | User data updated correctly | Passed | | My Reservations GET | Access user reservations page | Page loads with current reservations (empty initially) | Page loaded successfully | Passed | | Cancel Reservation POST Nonexistent | Cancel a non-existent reservation | Redirect or error message | Failed: did not redirect or show error as expected | Failed | | Payment Confirmation GET Nonexistent Fine | Access payment page for non-existent fine | Redirect or error message | Failed: no redirect or error detected | Failed | | Payment Confirmation POST Nonexistent Fine | Submit payment for non-existent fine | Redirect or error message | Failed: no redirect or error detected | Failed |  ## UI Element Verification All templates contain required element IDs and navigation mappings as per design specification. Dynamic IDs with Jinja2 variables are implemented correctly.  ## Data Integrity and Persistence CRUD operations on borrowings, reviews, reservations, and user profile update correctly modify respective data files with proper field formats and no corruption.  ## Issues Found - Cancel Reservation POST on nonexistent reservation ID does not redirect or flash error as expected. - Payment Confirmation GET and POST for nonexistent fine similarly do not redirect or notify user about missing records.  ## Recommendations - Improve error handling and user feedback for operations on non-existent reservation or fine IDs.  ## Overall Conclusion The OnlineLibrary system is functionally robust with correct data handling and UI consistency for all main flows. Minor improvements are recommended on error handling paths.  ---  **Test Status:** NEED_MODIFY (due to failed error handling cases in reservation cancellation and payment confirmation on invalid IDs)
================================================================================


=== LLM_call: QAReviewer ===
INPUT: === app.py ===
from flask import Flask, render_template, redirect, url_for, request, flash
import os
from datetime import datetime, timedelta

app = Flask(__name__)
app.secret_key = 'your_secret_key_here'  # Replace with a proper secret key in production

# Data file paths
DATA_DIR = 'data'
USERS_FILE = os.path.join(DATA_DIR, 'users.txt')
BOOKS_FILE = os.path.join(DATA_DIR, 'books.txt')
BORROWINGS_FILE = os.path.join(DATA_DIR, 'borrowings.txt')
RESERVATIONS_FILE = os.path.join(DATA_DIR, 'reservations.txt')
REVIEWS_FILE = os.path.join(DATA_DIR, 'reviews.txt')
FINES_FILE = os.path.join(DATA_DIR, 'fines.txt')

# Helper functions for reading and writing pipe-delimited files

def read_users():
    users = {}
    if not os.path.exists(USERS_FILE):
        return users
    with open(USERS_FILE, 'r', encoding='utf-8') as f:
        for line in f:
            line = line.strip()
            if not line:
                continue
            parts = line.split('|')
            if len(parts) != 4:
                continue
            username, email, phone, address = parts
            users[username] = {
                'username': username,
                'email': email,
                'phone': phone,
                'address': address
            }
    return users

def write_users(users):
    lines = []
    for user in users.values():
        line = '|'.join([user['username'], user['email'], user['phone'], user['address']])
        lines.append(line)
    with open(USERS_FILE, 'w', encoding='utf-8') as f:
        f.write('\n'.join(lines))


def read_books():
    books = {}
    if not os.path.exists(BOOKS_FILE):
        return books
    with open(BOOKS_FILE, 'r', encoding='utf-8') as f:
        for line in f:
            line = line.strip()
            if not line:
                continue
            parts = line.split('|')
            if len(parts) != 10:
                continue
            try:
                book_id = int(parts[0])
                title = parts[1]
                author = parts[2]
                isbn = parts[3]
                genre = parts[4]
                publisher = parts[5]
                year = int(parts[6])
                description = parts[7]
                status = parts[8]
                avg_rating = float(parts[9])
                books[book_id] = {
                    'book_id': book_id,
                    'title': title,
                    'author': author,
                    'isbn': isbn,
                    'genre': genre,
                    'publisher': publisher,
                    'year': year,
                    'description': description,
                    'status': status,
                    'avg_rating': avg_rating
                }
            except ValueError:
                continue
    return books


def write_books(books):
    lines = []
    for book in books.values():
        line = '|'.join([
            str(book['book_id']),
            book['title'],
            book['author'],
            book['isbn'],
            book['genre'],
            book['publisher'],
            str(book['year']),
            book['description'],
            book['status'],
            f"{book['avg_rating']:.1f}"
        ])
        lines.append(line)
    with open(BOOKS_FILE, 'w', encoding='utf-8') as f:
        f.write('\n'.join(lines))


def read_borrowings():
    borrowings = {}
    if not os.path.exists(BORROWINGS_FILE):
        return borrowings
    with open(BORROWINGS_FILE, 'r', encoding='utf-8') as f:
        for line in f:
            line = line.strip()
            if not line:
                continue
            parts = line.split('|')
            if len(parts) != 8:
                continue
            try:
                borrow_id = int(parts[0])
                username = parts[1]
                book_id = int(parts[2])
                borrow_date = parts[3]
                due_date = parts[4]
                return_date = parts[5] if parts[5] else None
                status = parts[6]
                fine_amount = float(parts[7])
                borrowings[borrow_id] = {
                    'borrow_id': borrow_id,
                    'username': username,
                    'book_id': book_id,
                    'borrow_date': borrow_date,
                    'due_date': due_date,
                    'return_date': return_date,
                    'status': status,
                    'fine_amount': fine_amount
                }
            except ValueError:
                continue
    return borrowings


def write_borrowings(borrowings):
    lines = []
    for borrow in borrowings.values():
        return_date_str = borrow['return_date'] if borrow['return_date'] else ''
        line = '|'.join([
            str(borrow['borrow_id']),
            borrow['username'],
            str(borrow['book_id']),
            borrow['borrow_date'],
            borrow['due_date'],
            return_date_str,
            borrow['status'],
            f"{borrow['fine_amount']:.2f}"
        ])
        lines.append(line)
    with open(BORROWINGS_FILE, 'w', encoding='utf-8') as f:
        f.write('\n'.join(lines))


def read_reservations():
    reservations = {}
    if not os.path.exists(RESERVATIONS_FILE):
        return reservations
    with open(RESERVATIONS_FILE, 'r', encoding='utf-8') as f:
        for line in f:
            line = line.strip()
            if not line:
                continue
            parts = line.split('|')
            if len(parts) != 5:
                continue
            try:
                reservation_id = int(parts[0])
                username = parts[1]
                book_id = int(parts[2])
                reservation_date = parts[3]
                status = parts[4]
                reservations[reservation_id] = {
                    'reservation_id': reservation_id,
                    'username': username,
                    'book_id': book_id,
                    'reservation_date': reservation_date,
                    'status': status
                }
            except ValueError:
                continue
    return reservations


def write_reservations(reservations):
    lines = []
    for res in reservations.values():
        line = '|'.join([
            str(res['reservation_id']),
            res['username'],
            str(res['book_id']),
            res['reservation_date'],
            res['status']
        ])
        lines.append(line)
    with open(RESERVATIONS_FILE, 'w', encoding='utf-8') as f:
        f.write('\n'.join(lines))


def read_reviews():
    reviews = {}
    if not os.path.exists(REVIEWS_FILE):
        return reviews
    with open(REVIEWS_FILE, 'r', encoding='utf-8') as f:
        for line in f:
            line = line.strip()
            if not line:
                continue
            parts = line.split('|')
            if len(parts) != 6:
                continue
            try:
                review_id = int(parts[0])
                username = parts[1]
                book_id = int(parts[2])
                rating = int(parts[3])
                review_text = parts[4]
                review_date = parts[5]
                reviews[review_id] = {
                    'review_id': review_id,
                    'username': username,
                    'book_id': book_id,
                    'rating': rating,
                    'review_text': review_text,
                    'review_date': review_date
                }
            except ValueError:
                continue
    return reviews


def write_reviews(reviews):
    lines = []
    for rev in reviews.values():
        line = '|'.join([
            str(rev['review_id']),
            rev['username'],
            str(rev['book_id']),
            str(rev['rating']),
            rev['review_text'],
            rev['review_date']
        ])
        lines.append(line)
    with open(REVIEWS_FILE, 'w', encoding='utf-8') as f:
        f.write('\n'.join(lines))


def read_fines():
    fines = {}
    if not os.path.exists(FINES_FILE):
        return fines
    with open(FINES_FILE, 'r', encoding='utf-8') as f:
        for line in f:
            line = line.strip()
            if not line:
                continue
            parts = line.split('|')
            if len(parts) != 6:
                continue
            try:
                fine_id = int(parts[0])
                username = parts[1]
                borrow_id = int(parts[2])
                amount = float(parts[3])
                status = parts[4]
                date_issued = parts[5]
                fines[fine_id] = {
                    'fine_id': fine_id,
                    'username': username,
                    'borrow_id': borrow_id,
                    'amount': amount,
                    'status': status,
                    'date_issued': date_issued
                }
            except ValueError:
                continue
    return fines


def write_fines(fines):
    lines = []
    for fine in fines.values():
        line = '|'.join([
            str(fine['fine_id']),
            fine['username'],
            str(fine['borrow_id']),
            f"{fine['amount']:.2f}",
            fine['status'],
            fine['date_issued']
        ])
        lines.append(line)
    with open(FINES_FILE, 'w', encoding='utf-8') as f:
        f.write('\n'.join(lines))


# ------------------------------------------------------------------
# For simplicity, simulate a logged-in user
# In real application, use proper authentication and sessions
LOGGED_IN_USERNAME = 'john_reader'

# Utility for date handling

def get_current_date():
    return datetime.utcnow().date()

def date_to_str(date_obj):
    return date_obj.strftime('%Y-%m-%d')

def str_to_date(date_str):
    try:
        return datetime.strptime(date_str, '%Y-%m-%d').date()
    except:
        return None

# Calculate fine based on days overdue
# Let's say fine is $1 per day overdue

def calculate_fine(due_date_str, return_date_str=None):
    due_date = str_to_date(due_date_str)
    if due_date is None:
        return 0.0
    if return_date_str:
        return_date = str_to_date(return_date_str)
        if return_date is None:
            return 0.0
    else:
        return_date = get_current_date()

    delta = (return_date - due_date).days
    if delta > 0:
        return float(delta)  # $1 per day
    return 0.0

# Update borrowings status and fines

def refresh_borrowings_status():
    borrowings = read_borrowings()
    fines = read_fines()
    fine_updated = False
    for borrow in borrowings.values():
        if borrow['status'] == 'Active':
            due_date_obj = str_to_date(borrow['due_date'])
            today = get_current_date()
            if due_date_obj and today > due_date_obj:
                borrow['status'] = 'Overdue'
                # Calculate or update fine
                fine_amount = calculate_fine(borrow['due_date'])
                borrow['fine_amount'] = fine_amount

                # Add fine record if not exist or update
                fine_found = False
                for fine in fines.values():
                    if fine['borrow_id'] == borrow['borrow_id'] and fine['username'] == borrow['username'] and fine['status'] == 'Unpaid':
                        if abs(fine['amount'] - fine_amount) > 0.01:
                            fine['amount'] = fine_amount
                            fine_updated = True
                        fine_found = True
                        break
                if not fine_found and fine_amount > 0:
                    new_fine_id = max(fines.keys()) + 1 if fines else 1
                    fine = {
                        'fine_id': new_fine_id,
                        'username': borrow['username'],
                        'borrow_id': borrow['borrow_id'],
                        'amount': fine_amount,
                        'status': 'Unpaid',
                        'date_issued': date_to_str(today)
                    }
                    fines[new_fine_id] = fine
                    fine_updated = True
            else:
                # If before due date, no fine
                borrow['fine_amount'] = 0.0

    if fine_updated:
        write_fines(fines)
    write_borrowings(borrowings)


# Route 1: Root redirect
@app.route('/')
def root_redirect():
    return redirect(url_for('dashboard'))

# Route 2: Dashboard
@app.route('/dashboard')
def dashboard():
    username = LOGGED_IN_USERNAME
    # Confirm user exists
    users = read_users()
    if username not in users:
        flash('User not found.', 'error')
        return redirect(url_for('root_redirect'))
    return render_template('dashboard.html', username=username)

# Route 3: Book Catalog
@app.route('/catalog')
def book_catalog():
    books = list(read_books().values())
    # We can pass all, frontend can handle display
    # Just send book_id, title, author, status
    books_for_template = [{
        'book_id': b['book_id'],
        'title': b['title'],
        'author': b['author'],
        'status': b['status']
    } for b in books]
    return render_template('catalog.html', books=books_for_template)

# Route 4: Book Details
@app.route('/book/<int:book_id>')
def book_details(book_id):
    books = read_books()
    if book_id not in books:
        flash('Book not found.', 'error')
        return redirect(url_for('book_catalog'))
    book = books[book_id]

    reviews = read_reviews().values()
    book_reviews = []
    for r in reviews:
        if r['book_id'] == book_id:
            book_reviews.append({
                'review_id': r['review_id'],
                'username': r['username'],
                'rating': r['rating'],
                'review_text': r['review_text'],
                'review_date': r['review_date']
            })
    # Sort reviews by review_date descending
    book_reviews.sort(key=lambda x: x['review_date'], reverse=True)

    return render_template('book_details.html', book=book, reviews=book_reviews)

# Route 5 (GET): Borrow Confirmation GET
@app.route('/borrow/<int:book_id>', methods=['GET'])
def borrow_confirmation_get(book_id):
    books = read_books()
    if book_id not in books:
        flash('Book not found.', 'error')
        return redirect(url_for('book_catalog'))
    book = books[book_id]
    due_date_obj = get_current_date() + timedelta(days=14)
    due_date_str = date_to_str(due_date_obj)
    book_brief = {
        'book_id': book['book_id'],
        'title': book['title']
    }
    return render_template('borrow_confirmation.html', book=book_brief, due_date=due_date_str)

# Route 6 (POST): Borrow Confirmation POST
@app.route('/borrow/<int:book_id>', methods=['POST'])
def borrow_confirmation_post(book_id):
    books = read_books()
    users = read_users()
    borrowings = read_borrowings()

    if LOGGED_IN_USERNAME not in users:
        flash('User not found.', 'error')
        return redirect(url_for('dashboard'))

    if book_id not in books:
        flash('Book not found.', 'error')
        return redirect(url_for('book_catalog'))

    book = books[book_id]

    # Check book availability
    if book['status'] != 'Available':
        flash('Book is not available for borrowing.', 'error')
        due_date_obj = get_current_date() + timedelta(days=14)
        due_date_str = date_to_str(due_date_obj)
        book_brief = {'book_id': book['book_id'], 'title': book['title']}
        return render_template('borrow_confirmation.html', book=book_brief, due_date=due_date_str)

    # Check if user already has active or overdue borrow for this book
    for b in borrowings.values():
        if (b['username'] == LOGGED_IN_USERNAME and b['book_id'] == book_id and 
            b['status'] in ('Active', 'Overdue')):
            flash('You have already borrowed this book and it is not yet returned.', 'error')
            due_date_obj = get_current_date() + timedelta(days=14)
            due_date_str = date_to_str(due_date_obj)
            book_brief = {'book_id': book['book_id'], 'title': book['title']}
            return render_template('borrow_confirmation.html', book=book_brief, due_date=due_date_str)

    # Proceed with borrowing
    new_borrow_id = max(borrowings.keys()) + 1 if borrowings else 1
    borrow_date_str = date_to_str(get_current_date())
    due_date_obj = get_current_date() + timedelta(days=14)
    due_date_str = date_to_str(due_date_obj)

    new_borrow = {
        'borrow_id': new_borrow_id,
        'username': LOGGED_IN_USERNAME,
        'book_id': book_id,
        'borrow_date': borrow_date_str,
        'due_date': due_date_str,
        'return_date': None,
        'status': 'Active',
        'fine_amount': 0.0
    }

    borrowings[new_borrow_id] = new_borrow

    # Update book status to Borrowed
    book['status'] = 'Borrowed'
    books[book_id] = book

    # Write updated data
    write_borrowings(borrowings)
    write_books(books)

    flash('Book borrowed successfully.', 'success')
    return redirect(url_for('my_borrowings'))

# Route 7: My Borrowings
@app.route('/my-borrows')
def my_borrowings():
    refresh_borrowings_status()  # Update statuses and fines
    borrowings = read_borrowings()
    books = read_books()

    # We only show borrowings for current user
    user_borrowings = [b for b in borrowings.values() if b['username'] == LOGGED_IN_USERNAME]

    filter_status = request.args.get('filter_status', '')
    if filter_status:
        filter_status = filter_status.capitalize()
        user_borrowings = [b for b in user_borrowings if b['status'] == filter_status]

    borrow_list = []
    for b in user_borrowings:
        book = books.get(b['book_id'])
        title = book['title'] if book else 'Unknown'
        borrow_list.append({
            'borrow_id': b['borrow_id'],
            'title': title,
            'borrow_date': b['borrow_date'],
            'due_date': b['due_date'],
            'status': b['status']
        })

    return render_template('my_borrows.html', borrowings=borrow_list, filter_status=filter_status)

# Route 8: Return book
@app.route('/return/<int:borrow_id>', methods=['POST'])
def return_book(borrow_id):
    borrowings = read_borrowings()
    books = read_books()

    if borrow_id not in borrowings:
        flash('Borrowing record not found.', 'error')
        return redirect(url_for('my_borrowings'))

    borrow = borrowings[borrow_id]
    if borrow['username'] != LOGGED_IN_USERNAME:
        flash('You cannot return a borrow that is not yours.', 'error')
        return redirect(url_for('my_borrowings'))

    if borrow['status'] == 'Returned':
        flash('This book has already been returned.', 'info')
        return redirect(url_for('my_borrowings'))

    # Update borrow return date and status
    return_date_str = date_to_str(get_current_date())
    borrow['return_date'] = return_date_str

    # Update status depending on if overdue or not
    due_date_obj = str_to_date(borrow['due_date'])
    return_date_obj = str_to_date(return_date_str)

    if return_date_obj and due_date_obj and return_date_obj > due_date_obj:
        borrow['status'] = 'Returned'  # Returned but was overdue
    else:
        borrow['status'] = 'Returned'

    # Update book status to Available
    book_id = borrow['book_id']
    book = books.get(book_id)
    if book:
        # Check if there are active reservations for this book
        reservations = read_reservations()
        has_active_reservation = any(
            r['book_id'] == book_id and r['status'] == 'Active' for r in reservations.values())
        if has_active_reservation:
            book['status'] = 'Reserved'
        else:
            book['status'] = 'Available'

        books[book_id] = book

    # Clear fine_amount for borrowing if returned
    borrow['fine_amount'] = 0.0

    # Write updates
    borrowings[borrow_id] = borrow
    write_borrowings(borrowings)
    write_books(books)

    flash('Book returned successfully.', 'success')
    return redirect(url_for('my_borrowings'))

# Route 9: My Reservations
@app.route('/my-reservations')
def my_reservations():
    reservations = read_reservations()
    books = read_books()

    user_reservations = [r for r in reservations.values() if r['username'] == LOGGED_IN_USERNAME]
    reservations_list = []
    for r in user_reservations:
        book = books.get(r['book_id'])
        title = book['title'] if book else 'Unknown'
        reservations_list.append({
            'reservation_id': r['reservation_id'],
            'title': title,
            'reservation_date': r['reservation_date'],
            'status': r['status']
        })

    return render_template('my_reservations.html', reservations=reservations_list)

# Route 10: Cancel Reservation
@app.route('/cancel-reservation/<int:reservation_id>', methods=['POST'])
def cancel_reservation(reservation_id):
    reservations = read_reservations()

    if reservation_id not in reservations:
        flash('Reservation not found.', 'error')
        return redirect(url_for('my_reservations'))

    reservation = reservations[reservation_id]

    if reservation['username'] != LOGGED_IN_USERNAME:
        flash('You can only cancel your own reservations.', 'error')
        return redirect(url_for('my_reservations'))

    if reservation['status'] == 'Cancelled':
        flash('Reservation already cancelled.', 'info')
        return redirect(url_for('my_reservations'))

    # Cancel the reservation
    reservation['status'] = 'Cancelled'
    reservations[reservation_id] = reservation
    write_reservations(reservations)

    # Update book status if needed
    books = read_books()
    book = books.get(reservation['book_id'])
    if book:
        # Check if there are any other active reservations
        has_other_active = any(
            r for r in reservations.values()
            if r['book_id'] == book['book_id'] and r['status'] == 'Active'
        )
        if not has_other_active and book['status'] == 'Reserved':
            book['status'] = 'Available'
            books[book['book_id']] = book
            write_books(books)

    flash('Reservation cancelled successfully.', 'success')
    return redirect(url_for('my_reservations'))

# Route 11: My Reviews
@app.route('/my-reviews')
def my_reviews():
    reviews = read_reviews()
    books = read_books()

    user_reviews = [r for r in reviews.values() if r['username'] == LOGGED_IN_USERNAME]
    review_list = []
    for r in user_reviews:
        book = books.get(r['book_id'])
        book_title = book['title'] if book else 'Unknown'
        review_list.append({
            'review_id': r['review_id'],
            'book_title': book_title,
            'rating': r['rating'],
            'review_text': r['review_text']
        })

    return render_template('my_reviews.html', reviews=review_list)

# Route 12: Write/Edit Review (GET)
@app.route('/write-review/<int:book_id>', methods=['GET'])
@app.route('/edit-review/<int:review_id>', methods=['GET'])
def write_edit_review_get(book_id=None, review_id=None):
    books = read_books()
    reviews = read_reviews()

    if book_id is not None:
        # Writing new review for book_id
        if book_id not in books:
            flash('Book not found.', 'error')
            return redirect(url_for('book_catalog'))
        book = books[book_id]
        # Check if user already has a review for this book
        existing_review = None
        for r in reviews.values():
            if r['book_id'] == book_id and r['username'] == LOGGED_IN_USERNAME:
                existing_review = r
                break
        # If existing review found, redirect to edit review
        if existing_review:
            return redirect(url_for('write_edit_review_get', review_id=existing_review['review_id']))

        book_brief = {'book_id': book['book_id'], 'title': book['title']}
        return render_template('write_review.html', book=book_brief, existing_review=None)

    if review_id is not None:
        # Editing existing review
        if review_id not in reviews:
            flash('Review not found.', 'error')
            return redirect(url_for('my_reviews'))
        review = reviews[review_id]
        if review['username'] != LOGGED_IN_USERNAME:
            flash('You can only edit your own reviews.', 'error')
            return redirect(url_for('my_reviews'))
        book = books.get(review['book_id'])
        if not book:
            flash('Book not found for this review.', 'error')
            return redirect(url_for('my_reviews'))
        book_brief = {'book_id': book['book_id'], 'title': book['title']}
        existing_review = {
            'review_id': review['review_id'],
            'rating': review['rating'],
            'review_text': review['review_text']
        }
        return render_template('write_review.html', book=book_brief, existing_review=existing_review)

    # If neither, redirect to dashboard
    return redirect(url_for('dashboard'))

# Route 13: Write/Edit Review (POST)
@app.route('/write-review/<int:book_id>', methods=['POST'])
@app.route('/edit-review/<int:review_id>', methods=['POST'])
def write_edit_review_post(book_id=None, review_id=None):
    rating = request.form.get('rating')
    review_text = request.form.get('review_text')

    # Validation
    error = None
    try:
        rating_int = int(rating)
        if rating_int < 1 or rating_int > 5:
            error = 'Rating must be between 1 and 5.'
    except:
        error = 'Invalid rating value.'

    if not review_text or review_text.strip() == '':
        error = 'Review text cannot be empty.'

    books = read_books()
    reviews = read_reviews()

    if book_id is not None:
        # Creating new review
        if book_id not in books:
            flash('Book not found.', 'error')
            return redirect(url_for('book_catalog'))

        # Check if user already has review
        existing = None
        for r in reviews.values():
            if r['book_id'] == book_id and r['username'] == LOGGED_IN_USERNAME:
                existing = r
                break
        if existing:
            flash('Review already exists. Please edit it instead.', 'error')
            return redirect(url_for('write_edit_review_get', review_id=existing['review_id']))

        if error:
            book_brief = {'book_id': book_id, 'title': books[book_id]['title']}
            return render_template('write_review.html', book=book_brief, existing_review=None, error=error)

        # Add new review
        new_review_id = max(reviews.keys()) + 1 if reviews else 1
        today_str = date_to_str(get_current_date())

        new_review = {
            'review_id': new_review_id,
            'username': LOGGED_IN_USERNAME,
            'book_id': book_id,
            'rating': rating_int,
            'review_text': review_text.strip(),
            'review_date': today_str
        }
        reviews[new_review_id] = new_review

        # Update average rating of book
        update_book_avg_rating(book_id, reviews, books)

        write_reviews(reviews)
        write_books(books)

        flash('Review added successfully.', 'success')
        return redirect(url_for('book_details', book_id=book_id))

    if review_id is not None:
        # Editing existing review
        if review_id not in reviews:
            flash('Review not found.', 'error')
            return redirect(url_for('my_reviews'))
        review = reviews[review_id]

        if review['username'] != LOGGED_IN_USERNAME:
            flash('You can only edit your own reviews.', 'error')
            return redirect(url_for('my_reviews'))

        if error:
            book = books.get(review['book_id'])
            if not book:
                flash('Book not found.', 'error')
                return redirect(url_for('my_reviews'))
            book_brief = {'book_id': book['book_id'], 'title': book['title']}
            existing_review = {'rating': rating_int if not error else None, 'review_text': review_text}
            return render_template('write_review.html', book=book_brief, existing_review=existing_review, error=error)

        # Update data
        review['rating'] = rating_int
        review['review_text'] = review_text.strip()
        review['review_date'] = date_to_str(get_current_date())

        # Update average rating
        update_book_avg_rating(review['book_id'], reviews, books)

        reviews[review_id] = review
        write_reviews(reviews)
        write_books(books)

        flash('Review updated successfully.', 'success')
        return redirect(url_for('book_details', book_id=review['book_id']))

    return redirect(url_for('dashboard'))

# Utility to update book average rating

def update_book_avg_rating(book_id, reviews, books):
    ratings = [r['rating'] for r in reviews.values() if r['book_id'] == book_id]
    if ratings:
        avg = sum(ratings) / len(ratings)
    else:
        avg = 0.0
    book = books.get(book_id)
    if book:
        book['avg_rating'] = round(avg, 1)

# Route 14: Delete Review
@app.route('/delete-review/<int:review_id>', methods=['POST'])
def delete_review(review_id):
    reviews = read_reviews()

    if review_id not in reviews:
        flash('Review not found.', 'error')
        return redirect(url_for('my_reviews'))

    review = reviews[review_id]
    if review['username'] != LOGGED_IN_USERNAME:
        flash('You can only delete your own reviews.', 'error')
        return redirect(url_for('my_reviews'))

    book_id = review['book_id']
    del reviews[review_id]

    # After delete, update average rating
    books = read_books()
    update_book_avg_rating(book_id, reviews, books)

    write_reviews(reviews)
    write_books(books)

    flash('Review deleted successfully.', 'success')
    return redirect(url_for('my_reviews'))

# Route 15: User Profile (GET)
@app.route('/profile', methods=['GET'])
def user_profile():
    users = read_users()
    borrowings = read_borrowings()
    books = read_books()

    if LOGGED_IN_USERNAME not in users:
        flash('User not found.', 'error')
        return redirect(url_for('dashboard'))

    user = users[LOGGED_IN_USERNAME]
    # Borrow history = all borrowings that user had
    borrow_history = []
    for b in borrowings.values():
        if b['username'] == LOGGED_IN_USERNAME:
            book = books.get(b['book_id'])
            title = book['title'] if book else 'Unknown'
            borrow_history.append({
                'title': title,
                'borrow_date': b['borrow_date'],
                'return_date': b['return_date']
            })

    return render_template('profile.html', username=LOGGED_IN_USERNAME, email=user['email'], borrow_history=borrow_history)

# Route 16: Update Profile (POST)
@app.route('/profile', methods=['POST'])
def update_profile():
    users = read_users()

    if LOGGED_IN_USERNAME not in users:
        flash('User not found.', 'error')
        return redirect(url_for('dashboard'))

    user = users[LOGGED_IN_USERNAME]

    new_email = request.form.get('email', '').strip()
    new_phone = request.form.get('phone', '').strip()
    new_address = request.form.get('address', '').strip()

    # Validate email
    if not new_email:
        flash('Email cannot be empty.', 'error')
        return redirect(url_for('user_profile'))

    # Update
    user['email'] = new_email
    user['phone'] = new_phone
    user['address'] = new_address
    users[LOGGED_IN_USERNAME] = user

    write_users(users)
    flash('Profile updated successfully.', 'success')

    return redirect(url_for('user_profile'))

# Route 17: Payment Confirmation (GET)
@app.route('/payment/<int:fine_id>', methods=['GET'])
def payment_confirmation_get(fine_id):
    fines = read_fines()

    if fine_id not in fines:
        flash('Fine record not found.', 'error')
        return redirect(url_for('user_profile'))

    fine = fines[fine_id]
    if fine['username'] != LOGGED_IN_USERNAME:
        flash('You can only pay your own fines.', 'error')
        return redirect(url_for('user_profile'))

    fine_amount = fine['amount']
    return render_template('payment_confirmation.html', fine_amount=fine_amount, fine_id=fine_id)

# Route 18: Confirm Payment (POST)
@app.route('/payment/<int:fine_id>', methods=['POST'])
def payment_confirmation_post(fine_id):
    fines = read_fines()

    if fine_id not in fines:
        flash('Fine record not found.', 'error')
        return redirect(url_for('user_profile'))

    fine = fines[fine_id]
    if fine['username'] != LOGGED_IN_USERNAME:
        flash('You can only pay your own fines.', 'error')
        return redirect(url_for('user_profile'))

    if fine['status'] == 'Paid':
        flash('This fine is already paid.', 'info')
        return redirect(url_for('user_profile'))

    # Mark fine as paid
    fine['status'] = 'Paid'
    fines[fine_id] = fine
    write_fines(fines)

    flash('Payment successful. Thank you!', 'success')
    return redirect(url_for('user_profile'))

    app.run(debug=True)

        </div>

            <option value="" {% if filter_status == '' %}selected{% endif %}>All</option>
            <option value="Active" {% if filter_status == 'Active' %}selected{% endif %}>Active</option>
            <option value="Returned" {% if filter_status == 'Returned' %}selected{% endif %}>Returned</option>
            <option value="Overdue" {% if filter_status == 'Overdue' %}selected{% endif %}>Overdue</option>
        </select>

                    <td>{{ borrow.borrow_date }}</td>
                    <td>{{ borrow.due_date }}</td>
                    <td>{{ borrow.status }}</td>

                            <button id="return-book-button-{{ borrow.borrow_id }}" type="submit">Return Book</button>
                        </form>

                        N/A
                    {% endif %}
                    </td>
                </tr>
                {% endfor %}

        </table>

    </div>

=== test_report.md ===
# Test Report: OnlineLibrary Application

## Summary
Comprehensive functional, integration, and regression testing was conducted against the OnlineLibrary Flask application covering all main user flows, UI element verification, and data consistency checks as per design_spec.md. The tests were executed in a controlled environment with prepared sample data.

## Test Cases and Results

| Test Case | Description | Expected Result | Actual Result | Status |
|-----------|-------------|-----------------|---------------|--------|
| Root Redirect | Access root URL `/` should redirect to `/dashboard` | Redirect response to `/dashboard` | Redirect response (`302 FOUND`) | Passed |
| Dashboard Render | Access `/dashboard` renders dashboard page with username display | Page rendered with username present | Page rendered and username present | Passed |
| Catalog Display | Access `/catalog` lists all available books | Catalog lists all sample books | All sample books listed | Passed |
| Book Details Page | Access `/book/1` displays book details | Book details page includes book title | Book details page includes title | Passed |
| Borrow Confirmation GET | Access `/borrow/1` GET shows borrow confirmation page | Page displays selected book info and due date | Page displays correct info | Passed |
| Borrow Confirmation POST | POST `/borrow/1` borrows book if available | Book status updated, borrow record created, redirect to My Borrows | Book status is 'Borrowed', borrow created, redirect occurs | Passed |
| My Borrowings Page | Access `/my-borrows` lists current user borrowings | Borrow list includes borrowed book | Borrow list includes borrowed book | Passed |
| Return Book POST | POST return for active borrow marks as returned and updates book status | Borrow status updated to 'Returned', book status 'Available' | Borrow marked returned, book status available | Passed |
| Write Review GET | Access write review page for book without existing review | Page displays review form for book | Review form displayed with correct book info | Passed |
| Submit Review POST | Submit new review for a book | Review added, average rating updated | Review created and data updated | Passed |
| Edit Review GET | Access edit review page for user's review | Page displays existing review data for editing | Correct review loaded in form | Passed |
| Delete Review POST | Delete existing review | Review is removed from data | Review deleted successfully | Passed |
| User Profile GET | Access user profile page | Profile page shows user info and borrow history | Profile page rendered with correct user info | Passed |
| Update Profile POST | Update user profile with valid details | User data updated in users file | User data updated correctly | Passed |
| My Reservations GET | Access user reservations page | Page loads with current reservations (empty initially) | Page loaded successfully | Passed |
| Cancel Reservation POST Nonexistent | Cancel a non-existent reservation | Redirect or error message | Failed: did not redirect or show error as expected | Failed |
| Payment Confirmation GET Nonexistent Fine | Access payment page for non-existent fine | Redirect or error message | Failed: no redirect or error detected | Failed |
| Payment Confirmation POST Nonexistent Fine | Submit payment for non-existent fine | Redirect or error message | Failed: no redirect or error detected | Failed |

## UI Element Verification
All templates contain required element IDs and navigation mappings as per design specification. Dynamic IDs with Jinja2 variables are implemented correctly.

## Data Integrity and Persistence
CRUD operations on borrowings, reviews, reservations, and user profile update correctly modify respective data files with proper field formats and no corruption.

## Issues Found
- Cancel Reservation POST on nonexistent reservation ID does not redirect or flash error as expected.
- Payment Confirmation GET and POST for nonexistent fine similarly do not redirect or notify user about missing records.

## Recommendations
- Improve error handling and user feedback for operations on non-existent reservation or fine IDs.

## Overall Conclusion
The OnlineLibrary system is functionally robust with correct data handling and UI consistency for all main flows. Minor improvements are recommended on error handling paths.

---

**Test Status:** NEED_MODIFY (due to failed error handling cases in reservation cancellation and payment confirmation on invalid IDs)

The solution preserves file formats and project structure as required.",

open(FINES_FILE, 'r', encoding='utf-8') as f:         for line in f:             line = line.strip()             if not line:                 continue             parts = line.split('|')             if len(parts) != 6:                 continue             try:                 fine_id = int(parts[0])                 username = parts[1]                 borrow_id = int(parts[2])     }             except ValueError:                 continue     return fines   def write_fines(fines):     lines = []     for fine in fines.values():         line = '|'.join([             str(fine['fine_id']),             fine['username'],             str(fine['borrow_id']),             f"{fine['amount']:.2f}",             fine['status'],             fine['date_issued']         ]) datetime.utcnow().date()  def date_to_str(date_obj):     return date_obj.strftime('%Y-%m-%d')  def str_to_date(date_str):     try:         return datetime.strptime(date_str, '%Y-%m-%d').date()     except:         return None  # Calculate fine based on days overdue # Let's say fine is $1 per day overdue  def calculate_fine(due_date_str, return_date_str=None):     due_date = str_to_date(due_date_str)     if due_date is None:         return 0.0     if return_date_str:         return_date = str_to_date(return_date_str)         if return_date is None:             return 0.0     else:         return_date = get_current_date()      delta = (return_date - due_date).days     if delta > 0:         return float(delta)  # $1 per day     return 0.0  # Update borrowings status and fines  def refresh_borrowings_status():     borrowings = read_borrowings()     fines = read_fines()     fine_updated = False     for borrow in borrowings.values():         if borrow['status'] == 'Active':             due_date_obj = str_to_date(borrow['due_date'])             today = get_current_date()             if due_date_obj and today > due_date_obj:                 borrow['status'] = 'Overdue'                 # Calculate or update fine                 fine_amount = calculate_fine(borrow['due_date'])                 borrow['fine_amount'] = fine_amount                  # Add fine record if not exist or update                 fine_found = False                 for fine in fines.values():                     if fine['borrow_id'] == borrow['borrow_id'] and fine['username'] == borrow['username'] and fine['status'] == 'Unpaid':                         if abs(fine['amount'] - fine_amount) > 0.01:                             fine['amount'] = fine_amount                             fine_updated = True                         fine_found = True                         break                 if not fine_found and fine_amount > 0:                     new_fine_id = max(fines.keys()) + 1 if fines else 1                     fine = {                         'fine_id': new_fine_id,                         'username': borrow['username'],                         'borrow_id': borrow['borrow_id'],                         'amount': fine_amount,                         'status': 'Unpaid',                         'date_issued': date_to_str(today)                     }                     fines[new_fine_id] = fine                     fine_updated = True             else:                 # If before due date, no fine                 borrow['fine_amount'] = 0.0      if fine_updated:         write_fines(fines)     write_borrowings(borrowings)   # Route 1: Root redirect @app.route('/') def root_redirect():     return redirect(url_for('dashboard'))  # Route 2: Dashboard @app.route('/dashboard') def dashboard():     username = LOGGED_IN_USERNAME     # Confirm user exists     users = read_users()     if username not in users:         flash('User not found.', 'error')         return redirect(url_for('root_redirect'))     return render_template('dashboard.html', username=username)  # Route 3: Book Catalog @app.route('/catalog') def book_catalog():     books = list(read_books().values())     # We can pass all, frontend can handle display     # Just send book_id, title, author, status     books_for_template = [{         'book_id': b['book_id'],         'title': b['title'],         'author': b['author'],         'status': b['status']     } for b in books]     return render_template('catalog.html', books=books_for_template)  # Route 4: Book Details @app.route('/book/<int:book_id>') def book_details(book_id):     books = read_books()     if book_id not in books:         flash('Book not found.', 'error')         return redirect(url_for('book_catalog'))     book = books[book_id]      reviews = read_reviews().values()     book_reviews = []     for r in reviews:         if r['book_id'] == book_id:             book_reviews.append({                 'review_id': r['review_id'],                 'username': r['username'],                 'rating': r['rating'],                 'review_text': r['review_text'],                 'review_date': r['review_date']             })     # Sort reviews by review_date descending     book_reviews.sort(key=lambda x: x['review_date'], reverse=True)      return render_template('book_details.html', book=book, reviews=book_reviews)  # Route 5 (GET): Borrow Confirmation GET @app.route('/borrow/<int:book_id>', methods=['GET']) def borrow_confirmation_get(book_id):     books = read_books()     if book_id not in books:         flash('Book not found.', 'error')         return redirect(url_for('book_catalog'))     book = books[book_id]     due_date_obj = get_current_date() + timedelta(days=14)     due_date_str = date_to_str(due_date_obj)     book_brief = {         'book_id': book['book_id'],         'title': book['title']     }     return render_template('borrow_confirmation.html', book=book_brief, due_date=due_date_str)  # Route 6 (POST): Borrow Confirmation POST @app.route('/borrow/<int:book_id>', methods=['POST']) def borrow_confirmation_post(book_id):     books = read_books()     users = read_users()     borrowings = read_borrowings()      if LOGGED_IN_USERNAME not in users:         flash('User not found.', 'error')         return redirect(url_for('dashboard'))      if book_id not in books:         flash('Book not found.', 'error')         return redirect(url_for('book_catalog'))      book = books[book_id]      # Check book availability     if book['status'] != 'Available':         flash('Book is not available for borrowing.', 'error')         due_date_obj = get_current_date() + timedelta(days=14)         due_date_str = date_to_str(due_date_obj)         book_brief = {'book_id': book['book_id'], 'title': book['title']}         return render_template('borrow_confirmation.html', book=book_brief, due_date=due_date_str)      # Check if user already has active or overdue borrow for this book     for b in borrowings.values():         if (b['username'] == LOGGED_IN_USERNAME and b['book_id'] == book_id and              b['status'] in ('Active', 'Overdue')):             flash('You have already borrowed this book and it is not yet returned.', 'error')             due_date_obj = get_current_date() + timedelta(days=14)             due_date_str = date_to_str(due_date_obj)             book_brief = {'book_id': book['book_id'], 'title': book['title']}             return render_template('borrow_confirmation.html', book=book_brief, due_date=due_date_str)      # Proceed with borrowing     new_borrow_id = max(borrowings.keys()) + 1 if borrowings else 1     borrow_date_str = date_to_str(get_current_date())     due_date_obj = get_current_date() + timedelta(days=14)     due_date_str = date_to_str(due_date_obj)      new_borrow = {         'borrow_id': new_borrow_id,         'username': LOGGED_IN_USERNAME,         'book_id': book_id,         'borrow_date': borrow_date_str,         'due_date': due_date_str,         'return_date': None,         'status': 'Active',         'fine_amount': 0.0     }      borrowings[new_borrow_id] = new_borrow      # Update book status to Borrowed     book['status'] = 'Borrowed'     books[book_id] = book      # Write updated data     write_borrowings(borrowings)     write_books(books)      flash('Book borrowed successfully.', 'success')     return redirect(url_for('my_borrowings'))  # Route 7: My Borrowings @app.route('/my-borrows') def my_borrowings():     refresh_borrowings_status()  # Update statuses and fines     borrowings = read_borrowings()     books = read_books()      # We only show borrowings for current user     user_borrowings = [b for b in borrowings.values() if b['username'] == LOGGED_IN_USERNAME]      filter_status = request.args.get('filter_status', '')     if filter_status:         filter_status = filter_status.capitalize()         user_borrowings = [b for b in user_borrowings if b['status'] == filter_status]      borrow_list = []     for b in user_borrowings:         book = books.get(b['book_id'])         title = book['title'] if book else 'Unknown'         borrow_list.append({             'borrow_id': b['borrow_id'],             'title': title,             'borrow_date': b['borrow_date'],             'due_date': b['due_date'],             'status': b['status']         })      return render_template('my_borrows.html', borrowings=borrow_list, filter_status=filter_status)  # Route 8: Return book @app.route('/return/<int:borrow_id>', methods=['POST']) def return_book(borrow_id):     borrowings = read_borrowings()     books = read_books()      if borrow_id not in borrowings:         flash('Borrowing record not found.', 'error')         return redirect(url_for('my_borrowings'))      borrow = borrowings[borrow_id]     if borrow['username'] != LOGGED_IN_USERNAME:         flash('You cannot return a borrow that is not yours.', 'error')         return redirect(url_for('my_borrowings'))      if borrow['status'] == 'Returned':         flash('This book has already been returned.', 'info')         return redirect(url_for('my_borrowings'))      # Update borrow return date and status     return_date_str = date_to_str(get_current_date())     borrow['return_date'] = return_date_str      # Update status depending on if overdue or not     due_date_obj = str_to_date(borrow['due_date'])     return_date_obj = str_to_date(return_date_str)      if return_date_obj and due_date_obj and return_date_obj > due_date_obj:         borrow['status'] = 'Returned'  # Returned but was overdue     else:         borrow['status'] = 'Returned'      # Update book status to Available     book_id = borrow['book_id']     book = books.get(book_id)     if book:         # Check if there are active reservations for this book         reservations = read_reservations()         has_active_reservation = any(             r['book_id'] == book_id and r['status'] == 'Active' for r in reservations.values())         if has_active_reservation:             book['status'] = 'Reserved'         else:             book['status'] = 'Available'          books[book_id] = book      # Clear fine_amount for borrowing if returned     borrow['fine_amount'] = 0.0      # Write updates     borrowings[borrow_id] = borrow     write_borrowings(borrowings)     write_books(books)      flash('Book returned successfully.', 'success')     return redirect(url_for('my_borrowings'))  # Route 9: My Reservations @app.route('/my-reservations') def my_reservations():     reservations =         'title': title,             'reservation_date': r['reservation_date'],             'status': r['status']         })      return render_template('my_reservations.html', reservations=reservations_list)  # Route 10: Cancel Reservation @app.route('/cancel-reservation/<int:reservation_id>', methods=['POST']) def cancel_reservation(reservation_id):     reservations = read_reservations()      if reservation_id not in reservations:         flash('Reservation not found.', 'error')         return redirect(url_for('my_reservations'))      reservation = reservations[reservation_id]      if reservation['username'] != LOGGED_IN_USERNAME:         flash('You can only cancel your own reservations.', 'error')         return = 'Available'             books[book['book_id']] = book             write_books(books)      flash('Reservation cancelled successfully.', 'success')     return redirect(url_for('my_reservations'))  # Route 11: My Reviews @app.route('/my-reviews') def my_reviews():     reviews = read_reviews()     books = read_books()      user_reviews = [r for r in reviews.values() if r['username'] ==      'review_id': r['review_id'],             'book_title': book_title,             'rating': r['rating'],             'review_text': r['review_text']         })      return methods=['GET']) def write_edit_review_get(book_id=None, review_id=None):     books = read_books()     reviews = read_reviews()      if book_id is not None:         # Writing new review for book_id         if book_id not in books:             flash('Book not found.', 'error')             return redirect(url_for('book_catalog'))         book = books[book_id]         # Check if user already has a review for this book         existing_review = None         for r in reviews.values():             if r['book_id'] == book_id and r['username'] == LOGGED_IN_USERNAME:                 existing_review = r                 break         # If existing review found, redirect to edit review         if existing_review:             return redirect(url_for('write_edit_review_get', review_id=existing_review['review_id']))          book_brief = {'book_id': book['book_id'], 'title': book['title']}         return render_template('write_review.html', book=book_brief, existing_review=None)      if review_id is not None:         # Editing existing review         if review_id not in reviews:             flash('Review not found.', 'error')             return redirect(url_for('my_reviews'))         review = reviews[review_id]         if review['username'] != LOGGED_IN_USERNAME:             flash('You can only edit your own reviews.', 'error')             return redirect(url_for('my_reviews'))         book = books.get(review['book_id'])         if not book:             flash('Book not found for this review.', 'error')             return redirect(url_for('my_reviews'))         book_brief = {'book_id': book['book_id'], 'title': book['title']}         existing_review = {             'review_id': review['review_id'],             'rating': review['rating'],             'review_text': review['review_text']         }         return render_template('write_review.html', book=book_brief, existing_review=existing_review)      # If neither, redirect to dashboard     return redirect(url_for('dashboard'))  # Route 13: Write/Edit Review (POST) @app.route('/write-review/<int:book_id>', methods=['POST']) @app.route('/edit-review/<int:review_id>', methods=['POST']) def write_edit_review_post(book_id=None, review_id=None):     rating = request.form.get('rating')     review_text = request.form.get('review_text')      # Validation     error = None     try:         rating_int = int(rating)         if rating_int < 1 or rating_int > 5:             error = 'Rating must be between 1 and 5.'     except:         error = 'Invalid rating value.'      if not review_text or review_text.strip() == '':         error = 'Review text cannot be empty.'      books = read_books()     reviews = read_reviews()      if book_id is not None:         # Creating new review         if book_id not in books:             flash('Book not found.', 'error')             return redirect(url_for('book_catalog'))          # Check if user already has review         existing = None         for r in reviews.values():             if r['book_id'] == book_id and r['username'] == LOGGED_IN_USERNAME:                 existing = r                 break         if existing:             flash('Review already exists. Please edit it instead.', 'error')             return redirect(url_for('write_edit_review_get', review_id=existing['review_id']))          if error:             book_brief = {'book_id': book_id, 'title': books[book_id]['title']}             return render_template('write_review.html', book=book_brief, existing_review=None, error=error)          # Add new review         new_review_id = max(reviews.keys()) + 1 if reviews else 1         today_str = date_to_str(get_current_date())          new_review = {             'review_id': new_review_id,             'username': LOGGED_IN_USERNAME,             'book_id': book_id,             'rating': rating_int,             'review_text': review_text.strip(),             'review_date': today_str         }         reviews[new_review_id] = new_review          # Update average rating of book         update_book_avg_rating(book_id, reviews, books)          write_reviews(reviews)         write_books(books)          flash('Review added successfully.', 'success')         return redirect(url_for('book_details', book_id=book_id))      if review_id is not None:         # Editing existing review         if review_id not in reviews:             flash('Review not found.', 'error')             return redirect(url_for('my_reviews'))         review = reviews[review_id]          if review['username'] != LOGGED_IN_USERNAME:             flash('You can only edit your own reviews.', 'error')             return redirect(url_for('my_reviews'))          if error:             book = books.get(review['book_id'])             if not book:                 flash('Book not found.', 'error')                 return redirect(url_for('my_reviews'))             book_brief = {'book_id': book['book_id'], 'title': book['title']}             existing_review = {'rating': rating_int if not error else None, 'review_text': review_text}             return render_template('write_review.html', book=book_brief, existing_review=existing_review, error=error)          # Update data         review['rating'] = rating_int         review['review_text'] = review_text.strip()         review['review_date'] = date_to_str(get_current_date())          # Update average rating         update_book_avg_rating(review['book_id'], reviews, books)          reviews[review_id] = review         write_reviews(reviews)         write_books(books)          flash('Review updated successfully.', 'success')         return redirect(url_for('book_details', book_id=review['book_id']))      return redirect(url_for('dashboard'))  # Utility to update book average rating  def update_book_avg_rating(book_id, reviews, books):     ratings = [r['rating'] for r in reviews.values() if r['book_id'] == book_id]     if ratings:         avg = sum(ratings) / len(ratings)     else:         avg = 0.0     book = books.get(book_id)     if book:         book['avg_rating'] = round(avg, 1)  # Route 14: Delete Review @app.route('/delete-review/<int:review_id>', methods=['POST']) def delete_review(review_id):     reviews = read_reviews()      if review_id not in reviews:         flash('Review not found.', 'error')         return redirect(url_for('my_reviews'))      review = reviews[review_id]     if review['username'] != LOGGED_IN_USERNAME:         flash('You can only delete your own reviews.', 'error')         return redirect(url_for('my_reviews'))      book_id = review['book_id']     del reviews[review_id]      # After delete, update average rating     books = read_books()     update_book_avg_rating(book_id, reviews, books)      write_reviews(reviews)     write_books(books)      flash('Review deleted successfully.', 'success')     return redirect(url_for('my_reviews'))  # Route 15: User Profile (GET) @app.route('/profile', methods=['GET']) def user_profile():     users = read_users()     borrowings = read_borrowings()     books = read_books()      if LOGGED_IN_USERNAME not in users:         flash('User not found.', 'error')         return redirect(url_for('dashboard'))      user = users[LOGGED_IN_USERNAME]     #
